essay: the issue whether the minor should be trained to reprocess waste by their parents or teachers is certainly a contentious one. parents hold dominance in practical lessons of recycling, while professionals can discipline kids to be more environmentally-friendly on the one hand, the home is a far more convenient spot to learn how to recycle domestic garbage in a pragmatic way. to illustrate, parents can provide their kids with the necessary tools while instructing them to accumulate rubbish as well as assisting to think creatively in terms what can be made from daily plastic bottles or packages, which demand years to decompose. for instance, as the majority of waste comes from domestic disposal, it is much more constructive to recycle them at home before they are discarded to landfill sites. on the other hand, not only parents can train children to have an eco-friendly lifestyle, but the school is also alternative place so as to enlighten pupils’ competency about biodegradable living and how to contribute to solve environmental issues. the well-informed teachers, whose specialization is ecology-oriented, such as geography or botany, can broaden horizon of the young with regards to recycling, for example. in above mentioned lessons, children could be taught how to switch to renewables, ranging from energy-efficacious devices through differentiating eco-friendly products. in conclusion, school can bestow children with more theoretical knowledge, whereas coming out of functional instructions, parents are much more superior. hence, both, i firmly believe, are equally paramount in terms of disciplining the minors to be environmentally sustainable.
question: Some people think that children should be taught at school to recycle materials and avoid waste. Other people believe that children should be taught this at home. Discuss both opinions and give your own opinion.

essay: these days modern technologies are becoming increasingly sophisticated. however, i disagree with those who feel that in the end all human workers will be replaced by robots. firstly, the absence of human touch makes it highly unlikely that genuine and deep emotional understanding can be formed. although ai systems can work faster and without a break, they can not evaluate emotions before making decisions. these systems always remain highly rational and practical, which, in turn, find it challenging to bring in emotions while interacting with human customers. to illustrate, when it comes to mental health consultation, it would be difficult for systems to see the real situation such as the customer's body language, facial expression, and non-verbal cues. the fact that not all jobs can be performed by ai proves the importance of potential human workers cannot be overlooked. secondly, replacing human workers with ai mostly can increase the risk of unemployment. although it seems to have great potential for technology development, it leads people to financial crises, which cause them difficulties to fulfil their basic needs such as food, accommodation, and education. this reality could even be worse with the effect of the number of crimes due to the lack of job opportunities for workers. even though the future is even more promising to have such a great potential technology, the opportunities still have to be balanced for ai and human workers. in conclusion, it seems to me that employing robots has a substantial drawback, not only can not evaluate emotions but also increases the risk of unemployment.
question: Some people believe that eventually all jobs will be done by artificially intelligent robots. To what extent do you agree?

essay: life is very precious, and many people die annually around the world as a result of reckless driving. most of the experts believe that imposing heavy penalties for unlawfully driving is the better solution, whereas others argue that there should be other methods. i believe that the first argument is a better method. however, there should be other actions too to produce obedient drivers. undoubtedly human errors can happen, and should not ignore. even though it is a human error, the driver should be punished, with a warning. these warnings can accumulate in a point system and could provide negative marks. heavy penalties will increase the stress of the drivers and ultimately lose confidence in driving. nevertheless, there are many scenarios of unlawful driving. to name a few, young drivers seek thrill while over speeding, bike riders enjoy riding on a single wheel, and few people even drive while they are drunk. as long as these offences are done deliberately, there should be heavy penalties to control this behaviour. these penalties can be in the form of a large sum of fines, suspending the driving license, and even imprisonment. however, all the drivers should be appropriately educated on road rules and regulations before providing a driving license. not only that, there should be a probationary period to observe a new license, and people who will be caught with driving offences should not be allowed to get a permanent driving license unless they go through a proper driving test again. in conclusion, much strick punishments should be imposed to improve road safety while the governments should take more precautions on educating the drivers on road rules and regulations.

essay: in the century of technological advances, there is a high demand for consumer goods in the market. therefore, many companies aim to manufacture a tremendous number of products to compete with their rival companies. however, while striving for more profit, ceos often overlook the negative impact of production on the environment, such as greenhouse gas emissions and soil acidification. these issues can be mitigated through restrictions on the production of non-essential consumer goods and the development of an optimal recycling system. first and foremost, the environment suffers from greenhouse gases - byproducts of the manufacturing process like methane, carbon dioxide, and other gases. these emissions contribute to global warming, resulting in rising sea levels, wildfires, and other natural disasters. to prevent the adverse effects of human activity, each country's production sector should limit its manufacturing levels. for instance, governments can impose restrictions on the supply of less essential consumer goods. undoubtedly, limiting production may lead to increased inflation and potential economic crises; however, preserving nature must remain a top priority. secondly, scraps from factories negatively affect the land and its components. discarded waste accumulates in vast landfills, leading to soil acidification. as a result, the soil becomes unsuitable for plant growth, disrupting the ecosystem. herbivores face food shortages (plants), and their predators, in turn, struggle to find prey due to these deaths, leading to increased competition for available resources. consequently, the entire ecosystem suffers, eventually leading to its destruction. to address this issue, people should develop a robust recycling system, which will reduce the volume of garbage and help neutralize the soil. in conclusion, humanity is an integral part of the living ecosystem, and environmental issues directly affect our surroundings. pursuing profit at the expense of the environment can have devastating consequences. greenhouse gas emissions and soil acidification pose significant threats to the ecosystem. therefore, addressing these problems by limiting production and establishing recycling facilities is of paramount importance for the well-being of future generations.
question:
The increase in the production of consumer goods results in damage to the natural environment.

essay: in present days, students have difficulties in paying attention to their school classes. as a result, academic days are becoming tougher for them. this essay aims to discuss the causes and methods to alleviate this problem. firstly, our children are very stressed both from their teachers and parents. obviously, in this competing world, they are overwhelmed not only with academics but also by fiercely imposed extra-curricular activities. also, the parents do not hesitate to blame their child if they did not score high marks during the exam. surely, such practices hinder the joy in innocent childhood lives and push them towards tension. as a result, they fail to pay enough attention at school. clearly, immediate solutions are required to help restore the mental and physical health of our pupils. both parents and teachers should take incentives. for example, amidst of studies, sports and yoga classes can be included in the syllabus which helps to stimulate their energy. furthermore, our homes should be friendlier and parents have to spend time more appreciating the talent of their dear ones than criticizing. such practices help them relive from stresses and thus improve concentration. therefore, the children should be raised with necessary duties and responsibilities, not by imposing early adolescent obligations on them. to conclude, it is a fact that our students are facing a lot of problems which prevent them from being attentive at school. this essay discusses the possible causes of such stresses and strategies which can be put forward to tackle them.

essay: finding ways to improve educational quality is often one of the top priorities in every education system. in some cultures, high school students are encouraged to give their opinions about teachers, but i believe that this can also give rise to lack of respect and discipline in the classroom. on the one hand, it is true that feedback from learners may contribute to an improvement in educational quality. in many cases, the level of comprehension of students relies very much not on the content of the lesson but on the way teachers conduct it. if, for example, the class is slow, it will be ineffective for teachers to teach too fast so that most students fail to retain the information. without the comments of students, it would be difficult to know whether the speed of the lesson is appropriate for the class, which may eventually impair the quality of the lesson. however, there are several drawbacks of allowing students to make comments and criticism on their teachers. firstly, teachers can be vulnerable to the negative words of students. many will feel that their efforts in delivering the lesson deserve praise rather than criticism or any form of feedback. this idea is commonly shared by teachers in the education systems of many asian countries. secondly, the classroom may be in chaos due to massive numbers of comments. opinions vary from students to students, and it would be impractical for teachers to work out a way of teaching that can satisfy all students in conclusion, it seems to me that encouraging high school students to comment about their teachers does not necessarily mean an improvement in education quality

essay: it is irrefutable that it is important to teach our children money management early in their lives .although, many may consider it a positive phenomenon, it is not a trend without drawbacks. we will discuss both sides of the coin before a reasonable conclusion is withdrawn. firstly, we will discuss positive aspect of this trend. to commence with, parents think it is a good habit to give children a small amount of money on regular basis. they will understand value for money and turn into more responsible citizens . moreover, they would also been exposed to situations where they have to save fortnight’s money to buy something essential. for example, if they want to buy new version of video games, they should save from their pocket money and spend that money to buy the desire product. on the flipside, the penny allotted at such small age could deteriorate their life . chances of involving into wrong doings are on higher side with other friends. so, until child gets matured and understand the ins and outs of life, even a single penny can drift him away from his academic paths. instead, they should be given money to participate in co curricular activities or other sports and events. to conclude, parents play an important role in children’s life. parents should keep a strict watch where their children are spending their pocket money. after analysing both the aspects, i am of the opinion that children should be given little amount of money occasionally. they should learn how to save and use hard earned money. it is important to teach them financial management in early years of their life.

essay: many people believe that all teenagers should be obliged to contribute to the local community on an unpaid basis since this would be beneficial for both individuals and society. i disagree that we should force all young people to do unpaid work and expect social gaining from it. in my opinion, most young people have already been burdened with much pressure from their curricula and extra-activities. academic performance-centered schooling is now as demanding as a full-time job since educators always push their students to catch up with the grind. otherwise, they might leave themselves behind with their studies. when teenagers can spare some time, they should be encouraged to engage in leisure pursuits. for example, participating in a sports team or joining an art class would equip them with opportunities to hone teamwork skills or excavate the art prodigy from within that could go along way in life. additionally, they have many years of working ahead of them after their graduation. at the same time, i do not believe that society would reap much from forcing teenagers to do unpaid work. it would be paradoxical to have young people do unsalaried work without letting them make their own decisions while other people doing the same work can get paid. doing this would trigger resentment among teenagers and their parents since it goes against an equal society's values. furthermore, without any remunerative encouragement, teenagers tend to opt out of responsibilities and do things in a lackluster manner, which would lead to counter-productive results. in conclusion, i disagree that ones with power should not force young people to do work without pay since that would not be beneficial as expected. instead, teenagers should be encouraged to work on a volunteer basis.
question: Some people think that all teenagers should be required to do unpaid work in their free time to help the local community. They believe this would benefit both the individual teenager and society as a whole. Do you agree or disagree?

essay: in this contemporary world, people have many routes to choose to develop themselves. some of them are in favor of working or travelling instead of attending university’s lectures. there are many merits and demerits of working instead of learning. first of all, people may find schools and universities are theoretical and costly. some cannot afford the cost of 4 to 5 years of studying. as a result, some students leave school and start finding a job. they can gain practical experiences while working. secondly, those students can soon become financially independent. however, because of lacking qualifications, the occupation they found will be manual jobs with minimum wages and no prospect of promotion. also, in this era of modernization, manual labour will soon be replaced by machinery. on the other hand, pursuing higher education will bring more benefits. when people attend universities, they will probably end up with a bachelor’s degree, which is a requirement for professional jobs with high salaries and chances of promotions. so, the investment in higher education will offset the amount spent on tuition fees. despite the less exposure to practical experience, students can still gain practical experience through internships in big companies. besides, college life can be an opportunity to meet and connect with different individuals as well as enlarge their network, which will be of great assistance in their future careers. in conclusion, we cannot bear the advantages that leaving school and having a job has but there are still a lot more to take into consideration when studying higher education.
question: Some school leavers travel or work for a period of time instead of going directly to university. What are the advantages and disadvantages for their study?

essay: unquestionably, there has been a gradual rise in the number of couples who decide to delay parenthood in this cutting-edge society. this trend stems from several culprits, and i lean toward its negative aspects overshadowing the optimistic ones. there are various reasons cited to justify the tendency of giving birth at a later period of life. the initial culprit lies in the fact that as the market is reasonably competitive, young adults delay fathering children with a view to devoting themselves to a sound foundation for their dream occupation. another significant reason is that experienced parents better raise youngsters. it is equally vital because older fathers and mothers are primarily more experienced, affluent, and more responsible for a child's upbringing. i am thoroughly convinced that the detrimental aspects of bearing the child at a preliminary stage outweigh its benefits. children born by more prosperous and experienced parents enjoy more incredible/more invaluable opportunities to benefit from a comprehensive academic education. by this trend, parents' first working years will be interrupted by pregnancy and childcare. nevertheless, delayed parenthood is undoubtedly associated with countless health issues that are far more crucial than the mentioned advantages. if aged couples decide to give birth, there are several severe illnesses in the prenatal and postnatal period, like excessive bleeding or being unconcerned for a long time after childbirth. if any maternal health problem arises, the family will be irreparably regretful. from what has been discussed above, we might conclude that delaying parenthood can be blamed for numerous factors. although several benefits are apparent, i suggest future parents consider its adverse aspects regarding women's and children's wellness.
question: Nowadays, more and more people decide to have children later in their life. What do you think are the reasons for this? Do the advantages of this trend outweigh the disadvantages?

essay: architecture is one of the cultural heritage that has to be protected and prevented from being damaged. in recent years, a number of citizens have been becoming become increasingly interested in discovering the history of their houses. in this essay, i will illustrate the underlying reasons for people exploring information about their houses and outline two ways in which the history of their houses can be researched. one primary reason is for maintaining the house. knowing the construction and materials of buildings can help the owner in terms of maintenance. one possible approach would be to ask local residents. they might have been living in the area for ages. moreover, they have a great deal of information. admittedly, some messages from local residents; however, there might be the wrong information because of hazy memories. therefore, people have to find evidence to prove the right history of those buildings. another reason why more people are interested in understanding their home’s past is to preserve the heritage of historic buildings. if people own a listed building which is inherited from generation to generation, they would have a responsibility to preserve it and prevent it from being damaged. in addition to the data from family, another way in which people can explore the history of houses is by doing research in the local libraries. normally, the history of city developments is preserved in the local library. people can find a lot of data including the commencement date of the buildings, builders and historical stories. by way of conclusion, asking local residents and doing research in local libraries are possible ways to explore the history of houses. however, the messages which are discovered by word of mouth still need to be verified.
question: In some countries, more and more people are becoming interested in finding out about the history of the house or building they live in. What are the reasons for this? How can people research this?

essay: when young people are choosing what subject to study at university, most of them hope they will find a great job upon graduation. in reality fresh graduates fail to find work in their field and some are even forced to take up below graduate level jobs. in my opinion, the root of this problem lies in the outdated higher education system that should be modernised. traditional university education does not prepare students for their first job for a number of reasons. one of them is that many programmes are too theoretical. it means that highly educated graduates lack simple skills required for employment, for example, the knowledge of it software. employers, therefore, prefer candidates with hands-on experience. another reason is that universities offer the many enough students for engineering and science programmes. as a result, the uk labour market has an oversupply of law graduates and deficit of engineers. a reform of higher education could alleviate many of the problems, but would require joint efforts from universities and the government. if universities worked closely with employers, they could teach students practical skills that companies look for. meanwhile, the government could develop guidelines on how many students' universities should recruit in each subject in accordance with market demand. in conclusion, a likely reason why many graduates cannot find a job in their field because university education might not have provided them with the right skill set or in-demand profession. i think that universities and the government could improve the situation by updating the number of students in each subject in line with the market demand.
question: Many university graduates connot find a job in their chosen profession. What factors may have caused this situation and what, in your opinion, should be done about it?

essay: a major concern which has been caused by the thriving manufacturing industry is environmental pollution. considering the various toxic by-products, i believe the prime cause of this problem lies in the production system itself. therefore, what the manufacturing process of businesses must undergo to solve this issue is governmental regulation. to find the root cause of the damage done to the natural environment of the manufacturing sector, it is worth looking at the chemical substances disposed of by the producing plants. one of the gases which are emitted due to fuel consumption is carbon dioxide. being affordable and offering financial prospects, fossil and nonrenewable sources of fuel appeal to a vast number of companies. another hazardous substance to take into account is the pesticide which is widespread among agricultural productions. leaking from farmland into water sources or being sprayed aerially, insecticides, as well as herbicides, contaminate human food, threaten marine fauna, and pollute the ocean. on top of that, plastics have gained much public attention due to their ability to remain as waste for thousands of years after being discarded. having these points in mind, the conclusion to solve the problem that i have arrived at is tougher rules and regulations enforced by the governments. if environmentally-friendly and renewable sources of energy have been invented, but are not taking advantage of by producing factories, governmental promotions such as tax breaks or financial incentives will go a long way towards a more sustainable economic development. one more law that can be applied is holding businesses who are doing harm to the ecosystem accountable. for instance, what the government can do is introduce stiff monetary penalties to them and require them to follow greener business practices, as in replacing plastic straws with biodegradable ones like those made of bamboo or metal. in conclusion, it is the byproduct of the manufacturing process that takes the blame for damaging the natural environment. in an attempt to address this issue, government and businesses going green will play the key role.
question: The increase in the production of consumer goods results in damage to the natural environment. What are the causes of this? What can be done to solve this problem?

disagree with this opinion. on the one hand, the government should allocate funds to artists. the reason is that art can improve people's lives. it can not only enrich people's spiritual world but also strengthen the cohesiveness of the people throughout the country. of course, this fund can also better inspire the artist's creative inspiration and make them better to achieve their personal value. the government should develop some art-related policies. the forms of art are diverse, and it is difficult for different kinds of artists to succeed, so these artists can better develop and contribute to the country only with the support of the government. on the other hand, it is not necessary for the government to invest a large amount of money in artists and it should use this money for more important things, such as technology. the development of science and technology can enhance the country's overall national strength and the country's competitiveness. at the same time, some people are likely to pretend that they are musicians or writers to get help from the state, which will lead to a serious economic burden on the country. as a result, the country’s fiscal spending will increase and income will decrease. in conclusion, although the government's help to all kinds of artists has many negative effects, this does not stop the development of art. at the same time, art is also very important for the development of today's society. actually, the success of artists means the development and prosperity of all kinds of art. therefore, in order to create a good cultural environment, the country should provide assistance to these artists.
question: Some people think that government should provide assistance to all kinds of artists, while some others think it is a waste of money. What is your opinion?

essay: across the globe, advertising during sports has been of widespread controversy. some individuals believe that it is a welcome development as it helps boost popularity of companies and reduce the financial burden of sports organisers while others think it act as a distraction during the sporting activity thereby making the process boring and tiring. this essay will discuss both views and give my stance. on the one hand, advertisement is a way of increasing the popularity of a product. it has been noted that majority of the populace are interested in sports(,) so companies use this medium to create awareness about their products. for instance, during the world cup, cocacola company advertised the various types of product they manufacture. in addition, organising sport programme can be expensive, so a consensus is made between the organisers and sponsors. this agreement will benefit not only the company but alleviate the financial burden on the organisers. on the other hand, promoting products during sports serves as a distraction to viewers. this occurs when sport lovers who are already engrossed in the game, (no need for comma)awaits the outcome hence any further broadcast outside what they are viewing keeps keep them on edge and in suspense. a survey conducted in mushin viewing center stated that majority of viewers became angry and violent during advertisements while watching the match between chelsea and manchester united in 2021. although, there are some disadvantages of this, i am of the opinion that the pros outweighs the cons as it allows people to be aware of some product that they never knew existed. in conclusion, while some people view advertisement during sport process as a welcome development, others believe it is a waste of time. this essay discussed advantages like improving awareness of company products and reduction of expenses for organisers. it also stated its role as distraction to viewers.
question: Some people believe that climate has the greatest effect on people’s way of life. Others believe that the economy of the region has the greatest effect. Discuss both views and give your opinion

essay: nowadays, thanks to the development of the economy and technology, consumer goods are massively manufactured. however, the synthesis of these products can bring negative impacts to the environment. the causes of this problem and corresponding solutions will be discussed below. one of the reasons for this issue is that people desire more and more merchandise, but green technology is not advanced enough. during recent decades, the wealth of people is significantly increasing, so people have more needs and wants in various products, such as computers and telephones. as a result, the supply must also soar to match the requests from the society. meanwhile, there are still insufficient resources that can drastically reduce the pollution of the surroundings during manufacturing processes. for example, a great deal of sulfur dioxide will certainly be formed when a factory produces toys. moreover, some non-degradable chemicals such as plastics can pose a threat to the earth, because the combustion of these substances can create an enormous amount of hazardous gas. eventually, the contamination and damage to the air and the land will be much more severe unless the production and consumption pattern changes. based on that, a feasible solution to this problem is that we should develop environmentally friendly technology. as we know, it is highly unlikely to alter people's consumption patterns. hence, we need to figure out some ways that can alleviate the damage to the environment. for instance, the use of hydropower for providing electricity to plants can lessen the demand for combusting fuels. additionally, companies can start to apply degradable materials for disposable products. if straws are made of materials that can be degraded, they are not required to be burned in the landfill. in conclusion, the underdeveloped green technology and the surge in demand for goods are the main reasons for environmental pollution. to tackle this problem, we need to invent technology that can benefit the environment.
question: The increase in the production of consumer goods results in damage to the natural environment. What are the causes of this? What can be done to solve this problem?

essay: modern days with the shift of lifestyle and priorities, many prefer to have children at a later age. it is uncommon to see women give birth at the age of 18, usually is after 30 years old. studies has shown there are several factors contribute to this trend, from higher living standards, different life priorities, and parents choose to be more emotionally prepared. in this essay the factors will be discussed followed with my opinion on this. as the world develops, living standards have become higher. consequently, this increases living expense. in order to make sure children gets a decent tertiary education many parents start saving for their children education fund early. this, is only a tip of the ice berg as children will incur other expense such as food, medical, clothes, etc. adding up all these, many modern young couples have family planning and priorities on reaching stable financial situation before having children. this is an inevitable impact as the economy grows and people try to make more money for a comfortable life. secondly, as society becomes more educated, both men and women spend longer time in formal education. by the time university students graduate, they are on average 25 years old. some of the more ambitious ones will choose to focus on their personal goal or career accomplishment before settling down on forming a family. one thing leads to another, couples choose to get married late will eventually starting the parenthood journey late. another key factor on why people choose to have children late is they prefer to be more matured, emotionally and mentally before their children come along. this is because many view having children as a enormous task in life, it involves a lot of coaching to the young ones and setting their future path correctly. hence, they prefer to take longer time for preparation. in summary, there are benefits as well as downsides of having children late. in my opinion, it brings more advantageous as it allows parents to be better prepared in terms of financial and emotional stability. it benefits both the parents and children.
question: Nowadays, more and more people decide to have children later in their life. What do you think are the reasons for this? Do the advantages of this trend outweigh the disadvantages?

essay: these days, the amount of money spent in space travel had been increasing to develop more new technology, and opponents believe that the money should invest in other sectors in order to fully utilize the investment. in my opinion, there are several reasons why i do not agree with the statement. first and foremost, space exploration has boosted scientific research. the money spent on space tourism is the key for scientists to invent new technology, which may also lead other industries to work with space industry on research and development. for instance, smartphones camera, sofware, and robots are develop from space travel sectors. besides, space exploration can create lots of jobs that helps millions of people around the world find jobs in space industry. to further illustrate, engineers, space designers, and trainers, all of which is in high demand. hence, when there are more job opportunities available in the space sectors, the unemployment will decrease, and thus boost the country economy. by contrast, some critics argue that the government should spent money in public sectors instead of space tourism. it is true that the money spent in public sectors such as transportation, maintance of public infrastructures, and education will benefit the mass. to quote an example, the government could utilize the money to enhance the education system so that more kids had the chance to get a better education. the money should also be spent in upgrading the public facilities like hospital, police station, and the train station, these could greatly improve the citizens quality of life. furthermore, these funds should be used in subsidize the low class people to help them at least get a decent meal, which could decrease the occurance of social issues in a nation. in conclusion, there are various methods for government to spent money either space industry or public sectors. however, i think the money spent in space travel is reasonable and governmentshould continue to invest as it is part of the contribution to the humanity.
question:
Some people think the money spent on developing the technology

for space exploration is not justified. There are more beneficial ways

to spend this money. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

essay: in the modern world, it is observed by some authorities and politicians that people have been preferring to be a parent later mid-ages when compared to the past. economic reasons and career ambition have caused that the people postponed having a child. i believe that having a child in later age has more advantages not only individuals but also for society. first of all, the human population has been increasing rapidly for a long time. due to the locations’ high density, the living cost has increased suddenly. moreover, the cost of having a baby is extremely high. so, most of the couples have decided to birth a child after they stabilize their economic power. on the other hand, especially after the idea of liberalism have been spreading all over the world since 1980, people have become more selfish and only focused on their career. for instance, if a woman has a baby at 30 years old, which is the most important age for her career, she has to pause her career for a minimum of 2 years. high population in big cities mostly decreases the quality of the education system. if a society has a low number of students, the teachers are able to teach them more efficiently and productively. in other words, to have selected and quality people increase the production and profitability around the country. moreover, to find a job is heavy for the citizens in the countries which have a huge population. if parents would choose to be parent later age, the population decreases, but at the same time, the unemployment rate will decline. on the other hand, the only disadvantage of this issue is a low workforce. but, after industry 4.0, there will be no need people to work in factories because of the robots and machinery powers would do more work than a human being. in conclusion, in this contemporary world, people tend to have a child in a later age if compared with their parents. i am strongly convinced that, thanks to this situation, the countries and individuals have been benefited in many ways as aforementioned.
question:
More people decided to have children in their later age than in the past. Why?
Do advantages of this outweigh disadvantages?

essay: there is a belief that competition in sports have benefits on young people's education whilst other opinions state there might be downsides. there is no doubt over-competitiveness may cause several issues for teenagers but it also enhances their ambition and resilience towards the world. on one hand, there are those who agree on how powerful the effects of sport are in our daily perspective, as truly helps to perceive more self-security, strength, and willpower. that means, teenagers will be capable to develop an instinct that enables them to face this challenging world we now live in. additionally, this “competitive mindset” encourages them to improve and pushes them forward. on the other hand, if this sense of competence is misunderstood in an unhealthy way, which basically means having a feeling of overpressure, troubles might appear. if they end up believing these games are a battle for power rather than a funny experience, the line has been crossed. aggressivity has a big role to play and it is definitely the boundary when conflict and arguments arise. i personally believe that, if competitive sports are well-focused and planned, they should not be a big deal. i think before or after each training a speech should be given in order to prevent conflicts between them. competitive and respect values that ensure peace and companionship are, without any doubt, essential lessons. to conclude, competitive sports could be either potentially useful or violent, the point is how we teach teenagers to practice them, and which principles are linked to it. it is probably true that, providing them an appropriate instruction, benefits could outweigh downsides.
question:
Some people think that competitive sports have a positive effect on the education of teenagers while others argue that the effect is negative.


Discuss both these views and give your own opinion.

essay: in modern age, cooperation has become more essential. some parents believe that their kids should join more team sports in school since it is the most effective way to train the students to cooperate with others well. i agree with this opinion to some extent. attending team sports at school has an crucial effect for educating the students about team work skill. first of all, it’s true that school is the place that kids spend almost of his time. specifically, the average time for children to stay at school is about 8 hours. thus, school is the best place for children to learn knowledge and make progresses. secondly, , it is always required that students should complete the homework independently that they rarely need to cooperate with classmates. if the duties for kids are listen teachers’ presentations and do the homework, they don’t have the opportunity to practise their cooperation. on the contrary, team sports is useful to educate the students how to deal with teammates. for example, if the players want to win, how could they don’t in the basketball games? however, as far as i believe, playing sports is not best and there are several better ways to improve the cooperation for children. on the one hand, there are a few drawbacks in playing sports at school. team sports are not suitable for every student. for instance, the thinner kids usually is not addict to score compared with the stronger ones. on the another hand, carrying out several projects by a group of students is better to learn how to cooperate. firstly, the situation in this case is more likely the situation in the work. it can help students know better what they should do in their future work. secondly, in the research students can get more academic information. to sum up, in order to get the progress related to cooperation, students not only should attend the sports at school, but also need to carry out the team work research.
question: The best way to teach children to cooperate is through team sports at school. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

essay: it is believed that the newspaper can have a huge impact on people’s thought processes. in my opinion, it has both pros and cons as the newspaper does provide us with the daily updates required for public awareness whereas, it can sometimes fabricate some incidents to mislead us. the way people perceive it made a lot of difference in identifying positive and negative implications. on the one hand, the newspaper plays a key role in passing information about what is happening in the world and the crisis situation, which can be used as a guide to take preventive actions needed. for instance, all relevant information related to covid outbreak treatment and preventive measures is getting published daily in the newspaper to help people combat it and its variants. even other health emergencies, such as a blood transfusion for rare blood groups like ab -ve or o -ve, are advertised in the newspaper, and the patient receives it on time with the help of a donor found from it. these can be some of the many benefits of reading newspapers. on the other hand, many stories related to crime or the personal affairs of celebrities are getting highlighted in some sections of the newspaper with made-up stories mostly to grab the reader’s attention and make it a sensation across the globe. many newspaper columnists add some spice to their articles to make it a selling point to the reader as the subtleness will not interest the readers. because of this, people involved in falsified articles have their image tarnished, consequently affecting their lives and their close ones with bad publicity. to conclude, people should not completely believe and perceive the news article and react to it. instead, they can rely on other news sources like television, radio, or online sources and then make up an opinion.
question: Newspapers Have an Enormous Influence on People’s Opinions and Ideas.

essay: artificially intelligent robots rather than people will do all of the jobs. while it is possible that artificial robots will perform all types of jobs except robotic technology. hence this essay will discuss why particularly all of the jobs will undertake robots than human beings. in these days, many jobs are undertaken by artificially intelligent robotic machines. cumbersome repetitive tasks are difficult for any people, therefore it is better to get it done by robotic machines. for example, all of the work in manufacturing foundries is done by robotic arms without any rest, which lead to maximum profit at large scale in a short span of time. for these reasons, robots will eventually replace almost jobs performed by humans in the upcoming future. conversely, artificial robotic machines cannot replace few jobs unless they learn that as well. human beings only know progressing in robotic technology, which outnumbers the artificial robots. for example, developing new algorithms for making artificial intelligent robots, which shall be developed by expert human beings. because of that major reason, it is in believing, that artificial robots due to their limited functional capacities will not do those jobs that require human knowledge. some people believe that artificially intelligent robotic machine will perform all of the jobs except those, which require complex level knowledge. all of the repetitive work may be handled by the robots, however, the people will do where the complex development is necessary. in my opinion, i believe that the robotic machines will perform all of the laborious jobs while the expert skilled peoples will do other jobs that require cognitive thinking ability.
question:
Some People believe that eventually all jobs will be done by artificially intelligent robots
what is your opinion?

essay: well, it is a matter of debate between groups of the crowd whether public support facilities are helpful for making folks fit. i personally believe that it gives an opportunity to society to improve fitness. let's discuss its pros and cons in the forthcoming paragraphs. firstly, an indoor stadium gives appropriate ambience to the community where they can enjoy games. youngsters go to the stadium for playing that would create chances to make teams and play friendly matches which help to keep people fit and healthy. for example, i live near the podium which is an indoor stadium and it is always full of crowed, i always go there for playing games like playing basketball and volleyball, i made new friends through these games as well as enjoy the game . during these games, i have lost my 5 kg weight within 3 months. apart from it, many children get coaching from professionals that may help them to become good players in the upcoming future. however, some communities are complaining because they get fewer results in their health. nowadays, everyone wants immediate results which are not possible therefore, people show less interest in these facilities. for instance, people need magic in their life, nobody is interested in hard work, like in advertisements, they show products which helps in weight loss programs. but the public doesn't think about their adverse effects on their health. moreover, some people play bowling that is just for fun, it doesn't take any physical strength. in conclusion, although people don't need sports health facilities, it is very important for keeping the public fit and healthy. they can enjoy their life if they are healthy and give they are a contribution to the growth of the nation.
question:
Some people say that public sports facilities improve people health problems. Others argue that this development has insignificant effects on public health.
Discuss both views and give your opinion

essay: there are controversial perspectives heating up a debate over how to live healthy. while scientists claim that there are a wealth of activities suitable with people’s health, some opposites still act bad activities. this essay is devoted to the underlying roots, followed by some equivalent effective solutions to this alarming problem. without a shadow of a doubt, a large number of useful advices from professional experts, however, people ignore them and have numerous harmful activities, especially smoking. this is because people have a tendency to use smoke in order to decrease stress when they have been under pressure in their lives. moreover, the low price of smoke is one of the reasons for this problem. for example, vietnamese man utilize smoke with high frequency from young to old age because of anxiety in their work and every smoke is fifteen thousand viet nam dong. hence, the price and the bad habit of user are common justifications. while the reasons are widely acknowledge, it is necessary to introduce far-reaching solutions for troubles. the government acts as precursor in minimizing this negative status. for example, the viet nam governor give some rules to limit it such as: increasing tax for smoke product and so on. moreover, the society projects are one of the most efficient resolutions. for instance, in thailand, tremendous state advert about the negative impacts of smoke to user and surrounding other with the aim of decreasing it. thus, rising smoke tax and advertising intensive effects of it should be done due to people’s health. in conclusion, it is vital for people to increase awareness of bad activities, especially smoking. in addition, the government and state have main responsibility for their residents’s well-being.
question: Scientists tell us some activities are good for health and others are bad. Despite knowing that million of people continue doing unhealthy activities. What are the causes and what are the solutions?

essay: there are a few individuals who believe that the prime objective of educational institutions is to transform their students to decent residents, instead of assisting them in becoming a better human being. in my opinion, schools should focus more on the former thought as better people create a better society. to begin with, in the past few decades, the educational system has changed drastically. this has changed in many ways which include what students are being taught in schools and universities. teachers play a vital role in the life of a student; hence they need to set the tone right in their classrooms. kids learn what their parents and teachers do, hence they need to take the conscious effort in doing good deeds. we need to teach good qualities to our kids such as good manners, self-confidence, being polite, so on a so forth, which will mould these students into better humans and take them to places. a nation will benefit if their citizens are well educated as well as if they are good knowledgeable workers . moreover, they can also add to the country’s economy. secondly, imbibing good qualities in the initial years of education will help them in the long run. furthermore, this will also reduce the number of crimes that occur in a country. to illustrate, educated population is less prone of committing crimes as compared to uneducated ones. it has been observed that states with higher levels of education have lower crime rate and vice versa. people would want to settle in a country where the crime rate is less as this will give them an emotional security. in conclusion, a nation should emphasize more on turning their citizens into better residents and great workers rather than profit them as individuals.
question: Some people think that the main purpose of schools is to turn children into good citizens and workers.rather to benefit them as individuals. To what extent do you agree or disagree ?

essay: these days, many countries use a huge amount of money on providing lessons related to pragmatic skills, such as video editing class and coding class, and the government insist that this will helpful for entire people. personally, i strongly advocate this view. in this essay, i will discuss the reason why i think this trend would be helpful with some examples. practical education should be more emphasized because whatever they learn through the practical way that knowledge will remain with them for a longer period. this ultimately increases the effectiveness of learning. for instance, when we are cramming a lesson for a test, our brain tends to remember it for a short while, which can be shorter than the duration of your test thus not serving the original purpose. while doing it practically, it can be in form of experiments, real-life projects, or educational trips, the knowledge and the whole learning experience stays in our mind for a long. so apart from bookish knowledge, practical education is very important for students. moreover, practical education through vocational training boosts a student’s self-esteem, knowledge, and skills, and enhances their transition into working life. with the competition for jobs increasing, employers are now seeking to hire candidates who can demonstrate their skills rather than reeling off a list of theoretical qualifications. ‘earn a university degree and get a job’, this formula has worked over many decades, but increasingly many fields today the formula is no longer working. for example, people who have maintained a good degree of computer literacy, especially coding a skill which cannot be obtained by conventional education, are very welcome in the job market. to conclude, it is clear that the benefits of practical learning outweigh the disadvantages, by providing more employment to people and enhancing the effectiveness of learning. thus, the government should support this learning system.
question:
Nations should spend more money on skills and vocational training for practical work, rather than on university education.
To what extent do you agree or disagree?

essay: there is no denying of the fact that for some people technology made life harder specially old people, although this is not he case for others. in my opinion, i disagree with the statement and i believe modern ways made growth more comfortable. in this essay, i will examine how machinery has affected the lives and will provide necessary illustration. to begin with, modern instruments made life much liveable and enjoyable, there are a plethora of examples for the same. in other words, with the help of gadgets complex jobs can easily be performed. for example, amul recently produced 100 thousand of dairy products which included manufacturing and packaging, it was done with the help of machines and in just 2 weeks. as a result, production these days are much easier and numerous types of products are available in the market. on the other hand, older generation find it hard to cope up with the technological advancements, what i mean is, as they new to this technology and they are familiar with orthodox ways, it is harder for them to use modern tech. for instance, my grandfather finds it really hard to use mobile phone and he often gets lost in the apps. consequently, aged people criticize the machinery and believe it just makes life complex. to conclude, technology is definitely shaping how the world works. younger people admire the automation and opines that it made life happier, while elders thinks it just problems are just replace and are not solved. however, i think if elder people take initiative and try to learn the aspects they will also agree that technology is a boon to humankind.
question: Some people think technology makes life complex, so we should make life simpler without using technology. Do you agree or disagree?

essay: because of the problems in transportation and accommodation confronted by cities today, some people propose that governments should encourage the relocation of businesses to the countryside. however, i believe that there are more drawbacks than benefits associated with this proposition. admittedly, moving large companies to regional areas is beneficial for urban districts. this helps in overcoming the problems that arise from the congestion of city centres by minimizing the migration of people from rural areas to cities and reducing the urban population. if governments implement this measure, several critical issues, including heavy traffic and housing shortage, will be addressed. however, this produces many negative effects. while employees can typically be hired at much lower salaries in the regional field, there is arguably a shortage of skilled workforce in these places. the majority of workers from outside city centres lack skills and experience, which can create trouble for companies and employees, such as project delays, understaffing, overtime work, and higher turnover rates. in addition, the infrastructure in the rural regions is not as developed, which can be problematic for certain companies. large businesses move to regional areas, expecting to operate as efficiently and effectively as they do in cities, but most of them end up struggling as they face such problems as lack of transportation, power outages, unreliable internet connection and scarcity of communication channels. in conclusion, more difficulties will be encountered by companies and individuals if governments push for the relocation of businesses from cities to rural areas. in my view, the advantages of such action are far outweighed by its disadvantages.
question: Accomodation and transport problems are increasing in many large cities. Businesses are encouraged to move to rural areas . Do the advantages outweigh the disadvantages ?

essay: while it is widely claimed that global warming is the most urgent environmental issue, some argue that deforestation has a more negative impact. both points of view and the reason why i believe that global warming has a pessimistic effect on the environment will be elaborated on in this essay. to begin with, it might seem sensible for some to believe that global warming has affected temperature all over the world, seeing that the increase in the emission of greenhouse gases including carbon dioxide and methane has contributed to the depletion of the ozone layer which has allowed more radiation reaching to the earth. to illustrate, numerous studies revealed that if the world’s temperature continues to rise at a higher rate than usual, it might cause an increase in sea levels, forcing an entire region to be disappeared. on the other hand, opponents of this idea might propose that deforestation should be taken into consideration because it has resulted in more carbon dioxide building up in the atmosphere and damaging effects on the environment, such as higher air pollutants. many countries, for example, are more likely to experience unhealthy air quality than a decade ago caused by deforestation. from this perspective, it is understandable why some would prefer deforestation to be the most pressing environmental issue. personally, i would argue that in favour of global warming crucially impacting on the sustainability in the environment. to simply explain, while the effects of deforestation might be limited to the area that has lost forests, higher levels of carbon dioxide from deforestation can actually contribute to global warming which is a phenomenon that affects the earth’s living beings. for instance, given that individuals incessantly release greenhouse gases from their actions, this could put the world at extinction risk. in conclusion, although it is undeniable that deforestation is an important environmental problem, i am of the opinion that global warming should be considered as the most pressing concern in order to mitigate the pessimistic effects of its environmental damages.
question:
While some people consider global warming to be the most pressing environmental problem now, others believe that deforestation has a more detrimental effect. 
Discuss both views and give your own opinion.

essay: in recent years, an increase in the manufacture of consumer goods has been seen. while this is connected to population growth, it has resulted in an increase in a detrimental effect to the environment. in this essay i will look at 2 possible causes for this as well as suggest some solutions. due to the extreme high volumes of manufacturers in order to meet the increasing demand of products, there has been an increase in factory produced co2 gases. this is because the manufacturing processes have not become more efficient as quantities have risen. in china for example, many new factories have sprung up over the last few decades resulting in huge quantities of pollution being fed into the air. if factories were to spend funding on researching more efficient manufacturing processes, the amount of polluted air would be decreased. manufacturers in their numbers, should concentrate on reducing co2 production. with the increase of factory workers, there has been a matching increase in the transportation of these workers to the facilities. in addition to this, there is an obvious rise in the transportation of products, all of which has resulted in higher carbon gas emissions form vehicles involved in the process. the number of busses and trucks carrying labourers to and products from the factories in china have added to the already polluted atmosphere in the country. improved and more efficient vehicles using no fossil fuels, such as in electric vehicles, or less fuel will improve this situation. transportation accounts for a large percentage of the worlds total carbon gas production. while it is understandable that we need more manufacturing facilities for the ever-increasing world population, we need factories who take environmental issues seriously. factory management need to ask themselves the question, "are we doing enough to minimize our carbon footprint?
question: The increase in the production of consumer goods results in damage to the natural environment. What are the causes of this? What can be done to solve this problem?

essay: in the world today people tend to give birth and start families at a later stage in their lives. from my own point of perspective, the merits outweigh the demerits because there might be unpalatable consequences resulting from this such as difficulty in conceiving a child. to begin with, despite these disadvantages, there are certain benefits which can be encapsulated under the umbrella of stability, both: financial and familial. as a couple progresses in life they should have greater financial resources to draw upon to support their children and reduce the negative impact on poverty. in addition to the financial stability, there’s also stability in the family which should increase as the couple matures. as a result, children born at a later stage are less vulnerable to the negative consequences of divorce and family break up which are less common in older pairings. although the positive aspects appear to have great strength, there are also significant negative factors to consider. foremost, as people age, they suffer from a greater amount of genetic degradation which can increase the chances of having a child with a genetic defect such as down's syndrome. another factor which can have a detrimental effect on a child’s quality of growth is the fact that older people tend to have less energy which means they are less able to cope with and raise their relatively more energetic young child. moreso, there is also a generation gap in terms of relatability. as the age gap between the child and the parents increases their ability to relate to each other and share the same ideas about life could be placed under severe pressure. this may have negative consequences on the child’s upbringing and development. for instance, there could be numerous intra-family conflicts between youngsters and their elderly parents. in conclusion, given the greater number of negative elements in comparison to the smaller number of positive aspects of giving birth at an older stage in life, it is reasonable to conclude that it is more beneficial to conceive and raise children at a relatively earlier age.
question: People nowadays tend to have children at older age. Do the advantages of this outweigh the disadvantages?

essay: the 20th century has evolved with various phenomena such as, the it, and industrial revolution, because of these developments there is a huge demand for skilled manpower. on the other hand, the courses, that are being offered by the universities are not revised since last 20 years; in addition to this, there is no professional certification course, such as python, java as part of syllabus; consequently, the companies do not hire the fresh pass out students. the university of technology, auckland, for instance, has not revised their courses since 1975, and during this year placements drive only 10% of the total students were hired, and the feedback given by the visiting companies was to revise the study modules. moreover, the professors are also not upgrading themselves with the latest developments, because they have to teach the same subjects, thereby students are not aware of the latest advancements in the modern world. the most suitable method to counter such situation is to, introduce certification courses mandatory for students and professors both. the students should pass at least one certification course to obtain the degree, whereas the teachers should obtain certification once a year to attain promotion to next level. a good illustration of this is that there was a record breaking placement at the university of kent, because most of the participating students were ccna or java certified. furthermore, colleges should form an association with the industries, so that their experts should visit the college campus to enlighten the scholars about the latest trends in the technical world. to conclude, to increase the employability, in my opinion universities have to catch up with the fast changing development trends. for this, they need to have a very close relationship with corporate, and also motivate students and professors to do professional certifications.

essay: with the virtual world connecting people more than ever, it becomes hard to notice the disadvantages of living in it. however, the virtual world is illusory and creates the impression of protection and stability, but only real-world interaction can give us true peace and confidence. admittedly, social networking reduces distances. at the moment, we can communicate in real-time with people thousands of kilometres away, creating connections and friendships. in addition, the news is more at our disposal and we can be up to date with any event whenever we want, such as the latest scientific inventions, the most popular commodities or the recent eye-catching lawsuits. however, when living in a virtual world, we are actually creating an artificial environment that we confuse with the real one. first of all, in this new world of virtual communications, we still express ourselves in real-world ways that don’t always work in this special context. examples can be seen when we sent an email or a text meaning one thing but the recipient took it in a completely different way. this is because online interaction lacks the emotions conveyed by our happy smiles, concerning looks or the tender touch of our hand. secondly, with the internet turning into an impressive power, many people have started to utilize it as a real source of income, promoting all kinds of fake news in exchange for earnings and increasing the energy consumed in filtering the noise. what’s worse, content-based algorithms are creating a ‘bubble’ where individuals got fed more of the same thing based on past searches or comments. consequently, by filtering out what the algorithms believe to be unattractive to their users, the social media platforms are in fact narrowing what the users could know. in conclusion, it seems that social media is opening lots of doors for its users. however, they are losing the insights, serendipity and surprises that early web users experienced before algorithms.

essay: with globalization, nations are more interconnected than ever before, giving rise to urgent and complex dilemmas on a global scale, some argue that international assistance is essential in addressing the majority of these pressing issues. i agree with this viewpoint. certain urgent problems can only be effectively addressed through collaboration with other countries. for instance, in the aftermath of a conflict, residents often find themselves grappling with injuries, homelessness, food and water shortages, and even threats to their lives. moreover, in such dire situations, local governments may lack the resources and capabilities to provide swift and comprehensive solutions. therefore, international support becomes paramount. whether through organizations or the governments of other nations, it plays a crucial role in safeguarding human rights. such help may involve rescuing the affected population, supplying essential resources, and offering overseas shelter to refugees. conversely, not all urgent problems necessitate external intervention, as seen in the case of crime rates. persistent and multifaceted concerns, like crime, are intricately linked to various domestic factors such as education, employment, and the efficacy of legal policies. consequently, the responsibility lies squarely with domestic authorities to institute long-term solutions rather than relying solely on international aid. nevertheless, seeking advice from other nations can serve as a valuable reference for improving local strategies. in conclusion, while international collaboration is undeniably a potent method for addressing urgent challenges, the capacity of local governments should not be underestimated. the dynamics of each issue determine whether it requires a global or domestic approach. striking a balance between international cooperation and local autonomy is essential for fostering effective solutions on a global scale.
question: Most of the urgent problems can only be solved with international cooperation. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

essay: housing, being a fundamental need for individuals, has sparked debates about whether the government should offer it freely for those who are economically disadvantaged. i wholeheartedly agree with this sentiment. i approve of the notion that free housing can lead to enhanced societal stability, which subsequently fosters national progress. in many societies, homelessness and housing insecurities plague the population, causing ripples of discontent and disenfranchisement. ensuring everyone has shelter, a direct impact would be a reduction in street crimes, as desperation often drives people to unlawful acts. for instance, countries with robust social housing programs, like denmark, report lower rates of vagrancy-related crimes. additionally, a stable living situation can enhance individuals' mental and emotional well-being, leading to more productive and content citizens. as more people become productive, it contributes to the broader development and prosperity of a nation. i concur that providing free housing can also result in a boost to economic mobility, paving the way for a more equitable society. when people aren’t burdened by exorbitant rents or the threat of eviction, they can allocate resources to education and skill development. this alignment of priorities leads to a more skilled workforce. for example, in nations where housing is heavily subsidized, such as in certain nordic countries, there is a higher percentage of the population pursuing higher education and vocational training. as these individuals climb the socioeconomic ladder due to their enhanced skills, it reduces wealth disparities and fosters a more harmonious and balanced society. on the other hand, i disagree with the proposition solely due to the potential strain on governmental budgets, eventually resulting in the potential neglect of other critical sectors. allocating funds for universal free housing would undoubtedly divert financial investments in other sectors like healthcare, education, or infrastructure. for instance, countries that have attempted large-scale free housing projects, such as venezuela, faced challenges in managing their budgets, leading to economic downturns. this financial strain can culminate in inadequate facilities, poorer healthcare systems, and compromised educational quality. the eventual outcome might be a society where housing is free, but other basic necessities and quality services become scarce or subpar. to sum up, while the merits of offering free housing are significant in fostering societal and economic growth, it is paramount to weigh these benefits against the potential strains on a nation's resources.
question: As housing is a basic need for people, the government should provide free housing for everyone who cannot afford it. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

essay: the recent increase in cases of obesity amongst humans is a cause for concern. it is often argued that this issue can be tackled by hiking the cost of foods that cause this problem. i completely agree with this point of view and therefore, in this essay, i will examine the reasons to support my opinion and provide a suitable conclusion. firstly, raising the price of unhealthy eatables makes it less affordable to the vast majority of the general public. this is because, since most people have a fixed budget to spend on food, they are less likely to splurge on costly snacks and thus they will consume it in very low quantities. for example, if a packet of potato chips cost thrice as much as it used to, by default an average person will look to buy only one instead of multiple packs. this is to say that the higher the price, lower will be the consumption. secondly, when the cost of commodities goes up, manufacturers will be forced to reduce production or even halt it. that is why when buyers cut down on buying their products, the company will suffer losses and hence they will be forced to stop releasing unhealthy edibles and maybe it can even encourage them to venture into making healthy snacks. in conclusion, changing the price of snacks that are bad for health will result in lower cases of obesity. i strongly believe making snacks unaffordable and forcing companies to stop producing foods that are harmful to health is the best solution to this problem.

increasing fuel costs will create a change in the transportation sector. many people will choose to take alternate mediums of transport. they will be encouraged to carpool, use public transport and even in the case of short-distance travel, walk or use a bicycle. however, there is still no guarantee for this. for example, in my hometown trivandrum, the price is currently inr. 104/- per litre, and keeps rising every week. considering, that travel by car and bike is the easiest mode of transportation because of the distance between the different requirements in the city, and the lack of a reliable public transport system, individuals are still forced to rely on their cars to move from place to place. alternately, what the government needs to do, is to strengthen the public transport sectors, impose heavy fines on the number of vehicles a family can posse, and in addition, they must thrust onto the public fines for using vehicles after a certain hour. furthermore, they should encourage local bicycle businesses, by creating more bicycle lanes and parks, which will in turn help to boost their business. car manufacturers, must look into the possibility of producing greener cars, and slowly phase out the production of petrol and diesel vehicles. once this happens, more and more petrol stations will cater to the new requirements by vehicle owners. these are some of the changes that the administration can enforce. in conclusion, i believe the increase in fuel price is not the only answer for this problem, but in fact, the government has to invest heavily in renewable forms of energy if any sustainable and lasting change needs to be made.

essay: the world today is extremely competitive when it comes to education, children and parents today are more aware than ever. many people believe that quality of education can be enhanced if high school pupils are motivated to question and provide negative feedback to their teachers when needed while the other group argues that this will impact the respectful relationship between students and the teacher along with causing the disciplinary problems in the class. this essay will talk about the opinions of both sides considering the advantages and disadvantages of the statement and then provide the author’s take on this. to begin with, getting constructive feedback from the student can help teachers understand what they could improve to raise the level of studies. if one teaching method works for 75% of the students, it doesn’t guarantee that it would provide similar benefits to the rest of 25% too. for example, a mathematical problem can be solved in many different ways, but that doesn’t make one process correct and the other one incorrect. similarly, if students are able to share what they think could benefit them, then the teachers will be able to make amends in their current teaching methodologies accordingly. despite the above benefits, it will give students a sense of power over their teachers which some might use in the wrong way, which can cause problems like disrespect and indiscipline. in fact, at a young ,age people are more drawn toward the wrong path when given powers like this. although feedback is necessary, it shouldn’t be allowed in the classroom in presence of all the other students. for instance, if a student feels that the current teaching methods are not helping him/her they should be allowed to speak to the teacher personally after the class. apart from that, the school committee should review and discuss the educational methods regularly in order to provide the best quality education. to summarize, the school council should be the one to review and update the ongoing educational ways. students should be allowed to share their problems, personally with the teachers as long as it doesn’t involve any disrespect and indiscipline.

essay: today, no one will deny the importance of protecting the environment. most scientists agree that global warming presents the greatest threat to the environment. there is little doubt that the earth is getting warmer. in the last ,century the average temperature rose about 0.6 degrees c around the world. many experts warn that global warming will cause sea levels to rise dramatically. in the last 100 ,years the oceans have risen 10 to 20 cms which may cause some coastal areas with lower elevations may be submerged. at the same time, climate change is likely to impact on ecosystems, agriculture and the spread of diseases."global warming is a serious threat to animal and plant life,' says jaymalcolm, a forestry professor at the university of toronto. as the climate warms, more southerly species will begin appearing further north, the species will find themselves in habitats where they don't belong. however what just caused this series of changes? just as the evidence is clear that temperatures have risen in the last century, it's also well established that carbon dioxide in the earth's atmosphere has increased about 30 per cent, allowing the atmosphere to trap too much heat. however, the exact link, if any, between the increase in carbon dioxide emissions and the higher temperatures is still being disputed. most scientists believe that humans, by burning fossil fuels such as coal and petroleum, are largely to blame for the increase in carbon dioxide. but some scientists also point to natural causes, such as volcanic activity. in conclusion, people need to be aware of the dangers of global warming and need to take some corresponding measures to deal with the possible harm
