essay: finding ways to improve educational quality is often one of the top priorities in every education system . in some cultures , high school students are encouraged to give their opinions about teachers . however l believe that this can also give rise to a lack of respect and discipline in the classroom . teacher evaluation from students on the one hand has certain advantages in honing educational quality . in particular, receiving feedback from learners offers instructors a chance for self-awareness and insight into their current strengths and weaknesses. thanks to studentsʼ comments . teachers may know whether their teaching methods are appropriate and then adjust their lesson plans to make the study period more enjoyable and effective . moreover , leaving comments on each lesson will engage students more in the education process and encourage them to be more responsible with their own studies. on the other hand , from my point of view, the new approach to teacher appraisal will result in several drawbacks . firstly , some trouble-making students are inclined to take advantage of the chance to criticize their seniors with negative words . therefore, such misbehaviour will possibly lead them to lose their respect for the instructors and then deteriorate the relationship between the two parties . secondly,it is obvious that every learner could have a different thought about educational quality and students are entitled to voice their own requirements, even to critique teachers . by way of my conclusion, it seems to me that encouraging high school students to comment concerning their teachers does not necessarily mean an improvement in education quality.

essay: some individuals suppose that the government should allocate financial resources to other impoverished countries while others believe that it is of paramount importance to focus on solving their domestic issues. from my point of view, i strongly agree with the proposal of determining national priority on internal problems. on the one hand, it is undeniable that cross-border support is a symbol of humanity. geographically, people are divided into different ethnicities but by nature, it will be the core of connecting features among individuals. thus, aiding the poor stems from the consciousness and heart of each resident and is not solely contingent on monetary contributions. not to mention that helping other nations to encounter financial crises would eliminate the chances of illegal migration which is detrimental to surrounding developed regions. for example, back to the covid-19 pandemic, with assistance from the whole world related to foods, vaccines,... etc, leading to a sustainable society as well as expressing humanity among several nations. nevertheless, i am of the opinion that concentrating on addressing domestic issues such as homelessness or unemployment should be of the utmost urgency in any country. to begin with, the financial fund from the government is regarded as a crucial means to reserve and maintain welfare schemes as well as social security. the smooth flow of how society works would generate a favourable environment for employment opportunities which accordingly increases the economic growth rate. furthermore, employment prospects, consequently, generate more personal budgets which people can use to afford decent accommodation decreasing the homelessness rate. in conclusion, although transnational support should be executed as a gesture of humanity, i still believe that the government should put the goal of tackling internal issues in a country to be the top priority.
question: Some people believe that the government should not spend money on international aid when they have their own disadvantaged people like homeless and unemployed. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

essay: housing shortages in large cities have become a serious issue, leading some to believe that only the government can provide a solution. i disagree with this statement as i believe that even if the government may have a primary role in solving this issue, others can also contribute significantly to tackling the situation. it is true that governmental action is essential to resolving the shortage of houses in major cities. one way in which this is achieved is through legislation and policy-making, whereby the government can implement measures to increase the supply of affordable housing. for instance, they can enact zoning laws to encourage development and finance the construction of affordable homes. additionally, the government has the legal power to ensure fair practices in the housing market and promote responsible development. for instance, they can levy higher taxes on the ownership of multiple properties, which discourages such practices and thus leaves more affordable houses for lower-income residents. nevertheless, i believe that the complexity of the housing crisis necessitates collaboration among multiple stakeholders, particularly private entities and nonprofit organizations. regarding the former, the private sector has resources that can be mobilized to facilitate the construction of affordable housing projects. thus, with appropriate incentives such as tax breaks or expedited permit processes, the government can involve private corporations in the construction and management of affordable housing units. nonprofit organizations and community groups also have a significant role in mitigating the housing situation. the reason is that they possess a deep understanding of local needs and can properly advocate for marginalized populations. this can be done by connecting vulnerable communities with financial backers and philanthropists who are willing to make substantial contributions to the building of a shelter for those at risk of homelessness. in conclusion, while the government may play a central role in tackling housing shortages in large urban areas, i disagree with the view that no other parties can contribute. in my opinion, the lack of housing for citizens can only be effectively addressed with collective efforts that involve private companies and community organizations.
question: Only government action can solve housing shortages in big cities. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

essay: it is evident that television has a significant effect on children. while it is hard to determine whether the length of time spent on tv programs or its content has more marked impacts on preschoolers, i concur that both sides influence the behaviors of the youth equally. on the one hand, watching television for a long period of time would exert a host of detrimental effects on children. firstly, the longer youngsters view tv programs, the higher the chances they would get addicted to it. as they solely seek entertainment from television, it is inevitable that those young watchers/audience would neglect their participant participation in outdoor activities, which makes them prone to sedentary-related diseases such as obesity, muscle degeneration, and high blood pressure, . excessive attention on paid to tv shows would also impair their communication skills. specifically, by spending too much overmuch time behind in front of the screen, the juvenile would be discouraged from real-life interaction with family members and the outside world, depriving them themselves of opportunities to socialize. this, hence, would hinder their social development and profoundly affect their adulthood in the long run. on the other hand, the consequence of children watching inappropriate materials would be also deleterious. since they could not distinguish between the right and the wrong, counter-educational programs which broadcast violence and pornography as well as negative modelings such as smoking, alcohol abuse, and swearing, could easily make children fall under the impression that those things are trendy. more seriously, they could end up imitating those inappropriate behaviors and adopting an unhealthy lifestyle. as an illustration, children who are exposed to violent shows and games at the early age not only often exhibit aggressive emotion and behaviors but also have a high potential of committing crimes, which poses a threat on the well-being of society. in conclusion, i am inclined to the school of thought that both the content viewed by children and the time they allocate for television would have implications on their conducts. when it comes to monitoring children‘s television viewing habits, it is imperative to provide timely parental guidance coped with stringent governmental regulations.
question: Some people say that what children watch influences their behaviour. Others believe the amount of time they spend on television influences their behaviour most. Discuss both view and give your opinion.

essay: businesses have always sought to make a profit, but it is becoming increasingly common to hear people talk about the social obligations that companies have. i completely agree with the idea that businesses should do more for society than simply make money. on the one hand, i accept that businesses must make money in order to survive in a competitive world. it seems logical that the priority of any company should be to cover its running costs, such as employees’ wages and payments for buildings and utilities. on top of these costs, companies also need to invest in improvements and innovations if they wish to remain successful. if a company is unable to pay its bills or meet the changing needs of customers, any concerns about social responsibilities become irrelevant. in other words, a company can only make a positive contribution to society if it is in good financial health. on the other hand, companies should not be run with the sole aim of maximizing profit; they have a wider role to play in society. one social obligation that owners and managers have is to treat their employees well, rather than exploiting them. for example, they could pay a “living wage” to ensure that workers have a good quality of life. i also like the idea that businesses could use a proportion of their profits to support local charities, environmental projects or education initiatives. finally, instead of trying to minimize their tax payments by using accounting loopholes, i believe that company bosses should be happy to contribute to society through the tax system. in conclusion, i believe that companies should place as much importance on their social responsibilities as they do on their financial objectives
question: As well as making money, businesses also have social responsibilities. To what extent do  you agree or disagree

essay: working part-time has been widely discussed among people. while some people argue that adolescents need to find part-time jobs, others indicate that letting youth work part-time is not beneficial. in this essay, i will discuss both views and explain why i think teenagers should not be encouraged to find jobs. to begin with, people encourage students to get into the workforce as there are a plethora of benefits. first of all, young people will acquire know-how by having real-life experiences. for example, they get to learn how to remain calm and resolve problems when they are confronted with problems after they experience similar episodes of conflicts with customers, colleagues or bosses. in addition, they have an opportunity to know themselves. for instance, a teenager who started to work as a cooking assistant because he/she loved to cook realised that he/she was very bad at cooking and began to find other occupations. on the other hand, other people do not let adolescents have part-time jobs for there are myriads of disadvantages. firstly, working can draw students' attention away from studying. it is undeniable that they can not concentrate on studying when they work hard and get exhausted. secondly, if teenagers had bad experiences such as exploitation, they will have a life-long trauma that will affect them negatively. for instance, if they became a target of bullying at the workplace, it will be hard for them to overcome this trauma and become a decent member of society. to sum up, whilst encouraging youth to find part-time jobs has positive aspects such as increased know-hows and finding their aptitude, it also entails negative aspects. these include interfering with students' studies and leaving them with life-long trauma. in my opinion, students should not be encouraged to work as disadvantages take precedence over advantages.
question:
In some countries people encourage teenagers to find part-time jobs. Some people agree and others don’t.
Review both sides and give your opinion.

essay: online learning is provided at several universities to substitute for traditional classrooms. despite some drawbacks of this development, i believe they are eclipsed by its advantages. on the one hand, there are two major drawbacks of distance-learning programs at tertiary education. first, studying via the internet often lacks face-to-face contact thus hindering students’ learning efficiency. through online platforms, classroom interaction is rather limited making it hard for students to ask for clarification or receive direct feedbacks from teachers. this, therefore, leads to their low level of lesson’s comprehension. second, attending e-learning courses requires considerable discipline from learners. students who tend to procrastinate or struggle with work-life balance may fail to complete the course’s requirements. on the other hand, i believe that the values are more significant than such disadvantages. to begin with, web-based classes offers learners considerable flexibility in terms of learning pace. with technological devices such as smartphones and laptops, students are able to engage themselves in self-paced studying, which means that they are allowed to study at their personal speed and level. unlike traditional learning, this new method enables learners to focus on areas they need to improve rather than following the same path as other peers. another benefit of virtual classes is that they are suitable for students from various backgrounds thanks to their accessibility. for instance, learners with restricted mobility such as physical handicaps or injuries are likely to opt for this form of learning as they have great difficulties in commuting to schools. for those who come from remote areas or stay far away from school campuses, learning through the internet is highly favorable. in conclusion, it seems to me that the merits of online learning programs at higher education systems are notable than its downsides.
question: Some universities offer online courses as an alternative to classes delivered on campus. Do you think this is a positive or negative development?

essay: a few people believe that adolescents must be encouraged to participate in unpaid social service during their free time as it would be beneficial for both youth and society. i disagree with this view as the benefits outweigh disadvantages. to begin with, if juveniles are required to volunteer for unpaid neighbourhood services, some people think that it would enhance their feeling of empathy towards mankind and nature. for instance, volunteer to visit the shut-ins and people in needs can help teens to have a grasp of the life of them. as the youngster is highly impressionable, working for the neighbourhood will turn them into socially responsible adults. so, for the benefit of the district and youth, teenagers must be encouraged to occupy themselves with voluntary jobs in their free time. however, children between thirteen and nineteen years of age are fragile and through neighbourhood task, they have to dig deep into the harsh realities of life such as hunger and poverty. this could adversely affect their young conscience and they would be carrying the the negative image of society in their minds throughout their lives. also, if the job is unpaid, there is a lack of motivation as well because if they are not paid for their efforts, they may get discouraged to participate in future. for example, many children are devastated when they interact with the deprived segment of the society and sometimes this leads to serious cognitive issues like depression and phobia of meeting new people. therefore, it should not be mandatory for the juvenile to take part in unpaid social work in their free time. to conclude, although unpaid public service is a noble act, youth should not be compelled to engage in it until they are ready because it exposes them to the harsh realities of life. hence, volunteering should be a choice and not an obligation.
question: Some people believe that teenagers should be required to do unpaid community work in their free time. This can benefit teenagers and the community as well. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

essay: in this contemporary world, education is a fundamental requirement for any individual and, as a result, masses learn discipline, humbleness and many other qualities with academic; however, few people assume that art is a necessary part for children at school while others argue that it is merely waste of time for adolescents. in this essay, i explain both of these aspects and give my opinion further in the ensuing paragraphs. on the one hand, art is always beneficial for juveniles because it enhances creativity among children and also increases mind power. moreover, after significant stress from an academic course, students can reveal their stress from art classes and it also increases their concentration towards major subjects. for instance, one of the surveys conducted in the usa reveals that there is 70% of students perform better after attending art classes and also increase their school grades in exams. on top of that, some pupils are born with natural talents and art classes can improve their talent and they can shape their talent to practise it, it can help to build their bright career in future. on the other hand, some pupils are not good in art classes and good in an academic study that time it is waste of time for them. art subjects such as music, painting and writing seminar are not interesting for them so that juveniles could not focus better on this subject, therefore it creates a difficult situation for them and also they should not perform well in both art and main subjects. to illustrate, one of the surveys taken in my home country india state that there is 20% student who is not good in art and it also damages their main course due to they could not handle pressure from both. to conclude, art is always an imperative subject for adolescents. in my opinion, schools should arrange subjects from the interest of pupils, thus it could not increase burden for students and they can also improve their art skills.

essay: the rapid advancement of globalization has led to the proliferation of similar stores and items across various countries. while some people view this as a favourable trend, others perceive it as a worrisome development. this essay explores both perspectives, highlighting the advantages and drawbacks of the homogenized trend worldwide and ultimately providing a balanced viewpoint. advocates of the globalization of shops and products argue that it brings several advantages. firstly, standardization enhances convenience for consumers who travel frequently or live in multiple countries. familiarity with brand names and product offerings across borders allows customers to make informed choices without the burden of cultural or linguistic barriers. secondly, uniformity often gives rise to improved quality control, as global brands strive to maintain consistent standards worldwide, ensuring customer satisfaction and loyalty. thirdly, the homogenization of production can contribute to economies of scale, resulting in lower production costs and more affordable prices for consumers. in this sense, the prevalence of the similarity promotes accessibility and affordability, ultimately benefiting consumers on a global scale. on the other hand, opponents of global sameness in shopping and production raise valid concerns. the saturation of international brands can result in the extinction of local businesses and traditional products. as global corporations dominate the market, smaller, locally-owned businesses struggle to compete, depriving communities of their unique identity and cultural heritage. furthermore, the standardization of goods may lead to a loss of diversity in consumer choices. while convenience is favoured by some, others may argue that the charm of exploring new and unique items diminishes as the market becomes saturated with the same offerings. this can induce cultural assimilation, eroding the rich and diverse traditions that make each country unique. in conclusion, the homogenization of shops and products is a multifaceted development that elicits diverse reactions. embracing its advantages while mitigating its negative consequences is crucial for ensuring a sustainable global market that celebrates diversity and fosters cultural preservation.
question: In many different countries, most shops and products become the same. Some people think it is a positive development, while others think it is a negative development. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.

essay: in various nations, prison sentence is a common punishment for offenders. while some hold the view that training is more beneficial than custody, the other disagree. in my idea, education has more benefit rather than other retributions. i will state the reasons for my view in this essay. the most compelling reason for holding to my view is that prison sentence is useless to prevent criminals from reoffending. firstly, no practical change would happen in their life as a prisoner. as committing crimes is a consequence of how people think and their mindset is, deep and real changes should be created in their thoughts to turn over a new leaf. it would not be achieved unless appropriate and adequate training schemes are considered. it can be really helpful for those who are enticed by others to become an offender. moreover, committing crimes is not only because of lack of education, but also because of being jobless that may lead people to some offences: smuggling, mugging and theft to earn money. if people learn a skill, their chance for finding a job will be increased. so, teaching a skill can make offenders to find a job and possibility of reoffending would be lessened in this situation. another reason for my position is that being in a jail has some drawbacks as well as physical and mental hurts and should be altered by another punishment such as education. despite all protecting measures, many diseases and injuries can be observed in jails. sometimes, health condition is not acceptable in prisons. nor can a wide variety of viruses and bacteria exist there. in addition, it is common in jails that criminals fall out with each other. so, some of them will be hurt. on the other hand, becoming homesick, depressed and disappointed is one of the hazards that can threaten prisoners mentally. in conclusion, prison sentence has some demerits that are really significant. however, many advantages can be achieved by training criminals.
question: In many countries, imprisonment is the most common solution to crimes. However, some people believe that better education will be a more effective solution. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

essay: it is true that large public facilities such as parks and other open spaces have beneficial effects on living conditions. however, i disagree with the idea that every urban area should have such spaces; some cities are obliged to tend to other much-needed infrastructure and service improvement. the presence of squares and parks certainly produces many benefits. firstly, these places promote exercise among city dwellers, bringing a positive change to their sedentary lifestyle. moreover, such green spaces regulate air temperature and absorb atmospheric pollutants, which helps reduce health risks inherent in many cities and towns. secondly, public venues can be used for important events and holidays, or as tourist attractions that help generate incomes and boost local economies. times square in new york city, for example, attracts millions of visitors a year, and it is considered one of the major economic drivers for the city. nevertheless, it would be impractical to make public spaces an absolute necessity because some cities are faced with more urgent issues which should be prioritised. as people move to cities for jobs, there is an urgent need for living spaces. therefore, instead of using lands for huge public spaces, city officials may use them to provide accommodation for the increasing population. also, constructing and maintaining public facilities can take up a significant portion of a city budget, which otherwise can be used for more pressing needs of urban residents. for instance, it is more important for some cities to build more hospitals or combat unemployment rather than create recreational places for the public. in conclusion, i do not believe that all cities need to invest in building public places as some have to allocate their space and financial resources for weightier matters.
question: It is important for all towns and cities to have large public spaces such as squares and parks. Do you agree or disagree with this statement?

essay: climate change has been the talk of the town these days, owing to the multiple challenges it posed to the globe and its adverse effect on the lives and livelihood of the people. many people argue that the ideal solution is to raise the fuel cost, that powers the vehicle. however, this essay disagrees with such a school of thought owing to more cons than its pros, since a surge in fuel prices is not a viable solution at all. to start with, the majority of people prefer private vehicles for their personal use, offices, schools, businesses, picnics, and so on. though it seemed logical to promote public transport while raising the price of power consumed by vehicles, however, this is not a systematic solution to the real problem. moreover, the larger and giant economies of the world should be held accountable for the worsening the climate of globe. more consumption of fossil fuels, like the burning of hydrocarbon by industries, the power sector, and an unending war of weapons, tend to increase the volume of greenhouse gases that are trapped in the atmosphere and ultimately increase the temperature of the planet. furthermore, a dedicated and committed approach to expanding the use of renewable energy is a feasible solution to the problem. in addition, the government must allocate funds for the transformation towards the cleanest sources of energy. alternatively, transportation systems can also make advancements in terms of fuel consumed like the introduction of lithium-ion batteries, and electric power vehicles. for instance, tesla company in the usa has recently gained a boost in their production of electric-powered vehicles. in a nutshell, owing to the scale of problems being faced by the world in the form of climate devastation, a sustainable and multi-layered approach is the only way forward to facilitate the people as well as promote green sources of energy.
question: Some people believe that one of the best ways to solve environmental problem is to increase the cost of fuel for cars and other vehicles. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

theories have been submitted to prompt researchers to argue that finding remedies for this issue will necessitate so much time, effort and money. however, the rising level of seawater, which stems from the climate change, is posing a major threat to mankind's habitable area. indeed, it is estimated that the proportion of land area dipped in water escalated from two per cent to six per cent in essay: there are growing numbers of men and women choosing to have children later in life these days. in my opinion, the financial advantages of this trend far outweigh any perceived downsides. the most significant tradeoffs of this relate to opportunity and maturity. many individuals decide early on in their career to wait until their mid to late 30s to have children. the natural risk here is that if the relationship ends before that point or they then have trouble conceiving, they may end up childless. this possibility is lower today due to advances in fertility science but still exists. furthermore, having children is a maturing experience. if an individual waits until late in life to raise a child, then they delay the experience gained and may later regret their decision. most parents would openly admit that parenthood is a life-altering milestone and defining moment of adulthood. nonetheless, the disadvantages detailed above pale in comparison to the economic merits of delaying childbirth. firstly, most young parents are not in an ideal situation in their careers. many working parents earn low salaries and work long hours. once they have a child that means the majority of their day is occupied and they may feel trapped and overburdened. it is then difficult to switch careers or move to a new location as well as afford all the expenses incumbent on parents. this often results in parents becoming resentful and projecting their animosity towards their children or significant other. in contrast, parents who are firmly established in their careers, earn decent salaries and have savings set aside have both the time and energy to devote to raising their children well without having to stress about making ends meet. in conclusion, despite marginal risks concerning the opportunity and experience, it is an overall positive for financial reasons that many prospective parents are putting off childbirth. therefore, this trend should be welcomed and encouraged.
question: People nowadays tend to have children at older ages. Do the advantages of this outweigh the disadvantages?

essay: due to easy access to the internet, many academic institutes, and especially universities offer virtual courses instead of in-person classes. it seems to me that this should be considered as a possible development. the main advantage of online programs is that the students can attend the class from wherever they are comfortable ; therefore, the transportation charges for coming to the campus every day will be reduced. also, the university doesn't have to pay extra charges for maintenance, power, etc. hence, both the university and the students can save more time and money. furthermore, these courses can be recorded and the students can rewatch the parts they missed during the class. they won’t be limited by the class hours and can study and work on the courses at any time of the day. since the sessions will be recorded, teachers will be more careful about what they say during their presentations and can minimize potential mistakes. online courses can be beneficial for the teachers too. they can stay at home and prepare for their lectures and have more free time for responsibilities besides work. on the other hand, holding classes on campus might include some other benefits. first, the key advantage of in-person courses is the physical interaction between the teacher and the students. this can affect learning significantly. moreover, asking questions and connecting with the class environment is less challenging in person and this help students to focus better and get on with the class easier. although the specific views on this issue may vary from person to person, i believe that the advantages of online courses outway the disadvantages and therefore, this should be considered a development.
question: Some universities offer online courses as an alternative to classes delivered on campus. Do you think this is a positive or negative development?

essay: it is true that spacious open areas such as squares and parks play a crucial role in all towns and cities. while i believe that this may bring certain benefits, i would argue that it is unnecessary for all urban areas to have these spaces. on the one hand, there are cogent reasons why the metropolis should build public zones for the sake of their residents. firstly, the city dwellers can reap the health of benefit thanks to public places. residents can enjoy capacious room to engage in extracurricular and recreational activities outdoors, which can guarantee a healthy lifestyle for surrounding people and deter them from potential health risks such as obesity or cardiovascular diseases. secondly, public areas may become a driving force behind the development tourism sector because of their distinctive beauty. for example, times square in new york city attracts millions of visitors to the united states annually, therefore, this can considerably contribute to income from tourism services. on the other hand, i believe that the appearance of these places is both nonessential and unsuited for part of cities. parks and squares construction appears to be a challenge due to the shortage of land in some urban areas. for instance, ha noi has a limited number of public zones, because the large spaces would be allocated for road system or accommodation to serve dense population. another disadvantage which should not be ignored is that public spaces require a huge number of costs such as running expenses and maintenance. this may be the culprit of the financial burden for numerous impoverished areas, where should pour budget into education, health care services instead of public areas. in conclusion, it is necessary for towns to have large public spaces but it also depends on each circumstances, whether or not the towns can afford them

essay: it is true that many nations allow parents the possibility to decide if their children will attend single-sex or co-education . some people argue that single-sex learning can lead to several obvious harmful consequences in the long run . in my opinion , i completely agree with this view and believe that the best choice for children is going to mixed-gender schools . on the one hand , studying at single-sex schools can help pupils to increase their academic performances and decrease the social distractions in the classroom . when boys and girls are separated , the gender attraction between students will be avoided and then no romance stories occurred in the classroom . as a result , pupils become more concentrated and do better at their studies and then they achieve good grades at the end of the year . moreover , the level of distraction at single-sex education become lower compared to co-education. consequently , the scholars waste less of their time which should be invested in meaningful activities and then they will have a better environment for studies . on the other hand , it is often argued that sending children to single-gender schools is not a realistic choice in the long run . firstly, learning in a mixed-gender environment can reduce sexism and improve gender equality .in other words , boys will learn to appreciate and respect girls and then they avoid the misogyny problems in the future . also , this way of learning will prepare them for real-life and build an equal society . secondly , co-education favorise the interaction between the two genders and then they will become more extroverted and audacious which are very important qualities for them when they would build a family and choose their partners . in conclusion , despite that single-sex education can bring some useful advantages to pupils , i believe that it is not a good choice for them because this way of learning will not help them to in their professional and personal lives .
question:

essay: recently, it is has become notoriously difficult for students to focus on their lessons. in my opinion, this is a partly natural phenomenon that has been exacerbated by technology and the solutions lie in strict restriction and supervision. the causes of a lack of concentration at school are both the pervasiveness of technology and human nature. firstly, people have always struggled to focus. very few students enjoy concentrating on tedious lessons and completing endless assignments at school. however, this has become worse as corporations exploit fundamental human characteristics. research has shown that the number of hours one spends on a laptop, smartphone, or tablet has a direct correlation with a shorter attention span. the reason for this is that technology supplies passive, immediate gratification. therefore, students cannot focus because of a combination of human susceptibility and predatory consumer electronics companies. the most feasible solutions are for parents and schools to monitor students strictly. this begins with parents. they must either approve or purchase a child their first smartphone or tablet and manage their early interactions with technology. many parents consciously gift young children these devices to help them learn how to use technology and aid their cognitive development. by waiting until later in life to approve a smartphone or limiting daily screen time, this issue can be greatly mitigated from the onset. schools also have a role to play as they must enforce strict rules banning the use of smartphones at school. for example, in many asian countries, students’ phones are confiscated if they are found using them during, or even between, classes. in conclusion, a lack of focus at school is driven by human nature and modern technology and parents and schools must work together to curb the worst excesses. government regulation would also help but should not be expected.
question:
Many students find it difficult to focus or pay attention at school nowadays.
What are the reasons for this?
What can be done to solve this problem?

clothes, footwear or utensils. as international brands penetrate every nook and cranny of society, the striking prevalence of goods from such brands will, in all likelihood, oust the traditional objects of a nation, which would be a disappearance of the richness and diversity of a country's local culture. furthermore, disillusioned with finding the shops and goods identical to those in places they once visited, tourists will find it to be big bea big letdown to travel abroad. in this case, easier access to the same products is more likely to hinder travellers from opening their wallets to buy specialities and souvenirs,thus impeding the blossom tourist industry. to recapitulate with retail homogenization, people are entitled to have easy access to products, whereas personally, such a faceless uniformity will occasion a dearth of cultural diversity.

essay: subject selection has always been a contentious topic among parents. while some of them believe that the study of art is pivotal in schools, others hold that it is meaningless to include art in the curriculum. in my opinion, art plays a vital role in children’s growth, which makes it irreplaceable in education. admittedly, subjects like painting or singing appear less academically critical guarantee well-paying jobs. for instance, those who learn to sing but cannot distinguish themselves from their peers may not achieve fame or recognition from the public. consequently, they might end up as music teachers or part-time singers with modest or unstable incomes. notwithstanding the potential drawbacks of art studies, the skills and pleasure derived from music, movies and books are incomparable. in specific, music can stimulate children’s language development and proficiency. research has demonstrated that children who spend more time listening to and singing music in their early years tend to develop better communication skills and excel in language use compared to their peers. furthermore, reading plays a vital role in nurturing creativity and imagination. when children read books like the harry potter series, they can envision the characters and the enchanting settings in their minds, fostering their creativity. artistic enjoyment and aesthetic experiences significantly contribute to psychological well-being. people suffering from mental illnesses can find solace in the soothing effects of listening to classic music. in conclusion, although the study of painting, singing or the arts may not equip children with the same level of academic as competitiveness as subjects like mathematics and science, it provides children with superior communication skills, enhances their creativity, and positively impacts their psychologically health. this demonstrates that art education is not a waste of time, but rather a valuable and indispensable component of a well-rounded education.

essay: some feel that the optimal solution to the current environmental crisis is to raise fuel costs for various private vehicles. in my opinion, though this would have a tangible impact, a more holistic approach is advisable. on the one hand, increasing the price of fuel would have a clearly identifiable impact. in both developing and developed countries, private vehicles are becoming more common. more expensive fuel costs would discourage individuals from taking long trips and encourage them to seek alternative modes of transport such as walking, riding a bicycle, sharing transportation, and using public transportation. the average family that struggles to pay their bills every month might then decide to stay home rather than take a short trip on weekends if the cost of travel outweighs the potential enjoyment. governments would be able to clearly chart the effects produced by these taxes and adjust them accordingly. on the other hand, a wide variety of measures that do not target less affluent segments of society would have a greater effect. a single remedy is unlikely to produce the desired result as the majority of middle and upper-class citizens would not be deterred by minor taxes. instead, the ministry should consider all the relevant environmental issues ranging from deforestation and single-use plastics to fossil fuel consumption in industry and the methane released in factory farming. to truly and sustainably counter these issues, authorities will need to employ a variety of tactics that will likely include not only taxes but also regulations and various educational initiatives. the tackling of a single cause, such as the fuel used in private vehicles, will have a marginal impact at best. in conclusion, despite the tangible results that a tax might engender, states should opt for a more comprehensive environmental policy. this is a pressing issue and requires a nuanced, long-term approach.
question:

essay: turning public parks and open areas in some cities into gardens so that locals grow their fruit and vegetables has become a common practice these days. although this trend does have some negatives, they are outstripped by a number of benefits it brings about. admittedly, there are a couple of drawbacks associated with changing parks and open spaces into gardens for growing food. the first one is that it is economically unviable to do so, because land in city areas is prohibitively expensive. produce obtained from these gardens, therefore, is likely to be a lot costlier than that from rural areas. another concern is the lack of places for people to socialize. public parks are the ideal spots for having a picnic, playing outdoor games and just taking a stroll. if they are used by locals to grow food, they it is likely that these places tend not to have enough room these activities, which is a possible cause of public frustration. that notwithstanding, i believe that the positive effects of this trend are far more significant than its negatives. this is firstly because people can have a chance to be involved in physical activities if they are to grow something. these include weeding, ploughing the land, cultivating and harvesting, all of which require some physical work, thereby allowing city dwellers to avoid a sedentary lifestyle. secondly, people can enjoy self-sufficiency when they have their own gardens. if put into good use, a fruit and vegetable garden can be a decent source of organic food. this, in turn, can help many people to reduce grocery expenses and provide them a sense of satisfaction for their efforts on land. in conclusion, transforming public parks as well as open areas into gardens might be disadvantageous for aesthetic and social reasons, yet i believe that these concerns are of secondary importance if we take health and economic benefits into account.

question: Children find it difficult to concentrate on or pay attention to their studies in school. What are the reasons? How can we solve this problem?

essay: many parents in vietnam choose to teach their kids at home instead of sending them to school. some children feel sad because they miss playing with friends, while others are happy to skip homework. i'll explain my thoughts on this topic. opponents of homeschooling say kids miss out on social skills by not meeting many people outside their family. this could make it tough for them in jobs or new situations. they also argue that homeschooled kids might struggle to get into college because they lack standard certificates. this could limit their opportunities for higher education. however, supporters of homeschooling see positives. they mention a child who excelled in physics because her parents taught her extensively. they also highlight the close bond between parents and kids when learning together, which strengthens their relationship. in my opinion, homeschooling has more positives than negatives. but for it to work well, parents need to provide recognized diplomas for their kids to enter universities.
question: In some countries, there has been an increase in the number of parents who educate their children themselves at home instead of sending them to school. Do you think the advantages of this outweigh the disadvantages?

essay: recent years have witnessed a debate about whether investing in encouraging people to stay healthy should be given more importance than saving the ill. while many patients are in demand of support from the government and community, i hold the belief that preventing illness should be prioritized over curing. on the one hand, it is a big challenge for the poor to resolve their health issues when living on a tight budget. this is the reason why there is a need for financial aid from the public. for instance, many children who are new-born have to deal with physical disabilities but their parents cannot afford to find proper treatment. as a consequence, in a real sense, a lot of fund-raising campaigns from not-for-profit organizations operate to implement the mission of bringing a better life for the unlucky. nevertheless, for a variety of reasons, it would be wrong if the emphasis is placed mostly on the treatment. instead, promoting healthy lifestyles will be of great significance in declining the illness. to begin with, many patients fall into a certain disease as a result of lacking knowledge and not being health-conscious. hence, there should be propaganda programs to alert people about the consequences of unhealthy lifestyles as well as how to stay away from dangerous illnesses. to illustrate, during the covid-19 period, the vietnamese medical authority implemented numerous communication campaigns to convey 5k regulations in order to raise people's awareness about how to stay safe during this pandemic, which brought a profound effect at that time. furthermore, encouraging a healthy lifestyle has a perennial impact on society. if most of the population has focused on health issues, thus, has better physical and mental well-being, the budget investing in treatment can be spent on other urgent problems like poverty or education. to conclude, although providing treatment support is necessary, i do believe that preventing is better than curing diseases. therefore, there is a need to plough ahead with social projects to establish healthy living habits.
question: It is more important to spend public money promoting a healthy lifestyle in order to prevent illness than to spend it on the treatment of people who are already ill. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

essay: in a happy society, having a healthy way of life plays a crucial role. because of this, a large amount of money has been spent to prevent diseases by encouraging a healthier lifestyle. however, some crowds argue that general finance should be used to treat patients who are suffering medical problems. from my perspective, while i agree to build healthy living, i believe that the treatment is equally essential. on the one hand, studies have shown that various illnesses nowadays are caused by an unhealthy way of living. this is because lots of diseases are the direct result of people’s habits. take an example of obesity, which can be improved by reducing the consumption of fatty foods or sugar, and increasing the intensity of exercise. furthermore, according to research by the cdc, promoting lifestyle modification is needed to control a range of diseases. as a result, the national burden of the diseases will be cut down. for example, the cardiovascular disease which is one of the most global burden diseases can be prevented through improving risk factors by the changes in lifestyle. on the other hand, numerous illnesses are unpreventable such as many forms of cancer, hiv/aids, some hereditary diseases, and so on. consequently, patients who are already contracted these medical problems need long-term treatment. taking into consideration, governments should come forward with a helping hand for those patients by implementing medical care policies. for instance, lots of free provide hiv medicines programs are supported by local authorities. in conclusion, both promoting a good lifestyle and spending money to treat patients who are being sick are equally important, and each country should develop flexible approaches to adapt to different situations.
question: It is more important to spend public money promoting a healthy lifestyle in order to prevent illness than to spend it on the treatment of people who are already ill. To what extend do you agree or disagree?

essay: due to limited availability of funds for higher education, it is often recommended that financial assistance should be offered to scientific research instead of allocating funds to supplementary subjects. this notion should be accepted as many facts in favour of practical subjects could be considered. certainly, the major argument in the favour of subsidising science and technology is rooted in the fact that advancement in science and technology not only reflects the prosperity of the nations but also vouchsafes the other domains. to illustrate, it can be said that investments in scientific research, including diagnostic and curative measures ensure the sound health of its people. secondly, it also paves a way towards the holistic development of a country by facilitating innovations and discoveries. another compelling reason might be due to unreservedly salience of science in terms of boosting the economy and living standards of the country. to be precise, a technically strong democracy can ensure the safety and security of the nation and stay connected to the rest of the world in order to enhance trade and contribute in elevating the financial status. for instance, owing to the unrivalled quality of equipment, japan is known as the world’s leading community. however, one might argue that the importance of supplementary subjects such as; economics and statistics should not be neglected as it assists the nation in policy formation and analysing the current situation. therefore, an equal proportion of financial assistant is required for these areas. from the analysis of the above discussion, one can observe that, evidently, government should pour more money in scientific research owing to its pivotal role in comprehensive growth of a state. nonetheless, it is pertinent that others domains should be taken into account while distributing the funds.
question: Money offered for post graduate research is limited; as a consequence, some people argue that financial support from the government should only be provided for scientific research rather than research for less useful subjects. Do you agree or disagree?

essay: there is a controversial opinion whether increasing the petrol price for motor vehicles is the most effective means of tackling environmental issues. in my perspective, i completely disagree with this opinion and there are two main reasons for this. in this essay, i am going to elaborate on the reasons. first of all, cars are an essential part of our current society, and increasing fuel fees could hugely impact our expenses and threaten our lives. this is especially evident in rural areas because insufficient public transportation is not uncommon in these areas, and they usually rely on motor vehicles for their transportation. furthermore, the main industry in these areas tends to be farming, which requires them to consume more petrol to run farming machineries. therefore, raising petrol fees can be a life-threatening way although there is potential to alleviate current environmental problems. secondly, the emission of exhaust gas from motor vehicles is not only the source of carbon footprint. for instance, coal plants to produce electricity are a large contributor to the increase in carbon dioxide, leading to climate change and rise in sea level. not only this but also having cattle for our food is said to be one of the largest sources of carbon dioxide emission. these issues should be considered along with reducing car emission gases. in summary, increasing fuel fees has enormous effects on our lives and, this is not the primary source of detridamaging nature. thus, other measures to improve environmental issues and protect nature should be addressed first.
question:
Some people think that one of the best ways to solve environmental problems is to increase the cost of fuels for cars and other vehicles.
To what extent do you agree or disagree?

essay: it is an unfortunate reality of today's world that majority adore and praise media personalities, not for their accomplishments, but merely for their charm and riches. i agree that this view certainly has an adverse impact on gullible youth, in particular, who believes such worldly possessions as the ace of human achievement. however, there are exceptions to it. this essay sheds some light on how young minds perceive success, idealizing celebrities while many amongst them lack the qualities to look up to. firstly, youth forgets that there is no shortcut to fame and fortune, rather they are the outcomes of a strong determination, disciplined lifestyle and endless dedication. celebrities’ real artistry should be inspirational to the young generations, whereas, youth only fantasizes their extravagant lifestyle and unrealistic beauty standards. consequently, a person with wealth and beauty becomes an idol for many without having any righteous personality. for instance, disregarding social and charitable work of the superstars is definitely a wrong possession of their true personality which sets substandard inspiration for youngsters. on the other hand, not all adolescents are credulous. they consider the blood, sweat and tears behind the wealth and glamour. for them a mega star is an epitome of struggle, perseverance and achievement. for example, mary kom, the renowned world champion boxer might lack glamour and wealth compared to other celebs, but she is an honourable inspiration to youngsters. in conclusion, having analysed how worldly possessions, can affect minors’ perception towards success, it is proven that this does set a faulty example. hence, minors should be encouraged to realise that, a man's worth should not be judged by his possessions, rather by the journey he goes through to achieve those.
question:
Nowadays celebrities are more famous for their glamour and wealth rather than for their achievements, and this sets a bad example to young people.

essay: interviews are a common selection method for many companies, but they are not the only method. other methods such as skills tests, reference checks, and work samples may provide a more accurate assessment of a candidate's abilities and fit for the job. while interviews can give an initial impression of a candidate, they may not provide a complete picture of their skills, personality, and work style. interviews may also be subject to biases, such as unconscious biases or interviewer biases, which can affect the outcome of the selection process. additionally, some candidates may perform well in an interview but not be a good fit for the job, while others may not perform as well in an interview but have the necessary skills and qualities for the job. therefore, while interviews can be a useful selection method, they should not be relied on solely to make hiring decisions. combining interviews with other selection methods may provide a more comprehensive assessment of candidates and increase the chances of making a successful hire.
question: Interviews form the basic selection criteria for most large companies. However, some poeple think that interviews is not a reliable mthod of choosing whom to employ and there are other better methods. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

question:

essay: the progresses of postgraduate research are highly dependent on financial support from governments. i agree that instead of investing in research that barely contributes to the world in a good way, money should go to subjects that could provide solutions for difficulties nowadays. to begin with, one of the cogent reasons why governments should put the majority of money into certain postgraduate research, including science and medicine, is due to professional areas as such playing essential roles to tackle various issues which need to be fixed urgently. for example, researchers in science are keen to use clear energies, including solar power, or hydroelectricity, to replace traditional power supplies, such as thermal power. that is why compared to literature and history, science research provides more practical usage. secondly, it could cost a tidy sum to support the process of research, be it upgrading equipment for experiments, or maintenance fee. furthermore, some subjects need participants to be involved for a long term, which will provoke a great among of spending. with limited budgets, correlative departments should make good use of it by carefully evaluating research for their potential and practical usage in the world. for example, medical research could provide solutions for rare diseases, which could enhance the life span of human beings. without financial support from governments such progress would be hard to achieve.  to conclude, i believe it is necessary for governments to support postgraduate research that could contribute to the world the most, despite that they might need to trade in the opportunities for other professional filed. i agree that governments should provide more financial subsidies to encourage certain postgraduate research to provide useful solutions.
question: money offered for postgraduate research is limited; as a consequence, some people argue that financial support from the government should only be provided for scientific research rather than research for less useful subjects. do you agree or disagree?

essay: there are controversial notions heating up the debate over the fact that news is not related to people’s lives which means it is considered a time-wasting to follow news through the media. although this thinking is valid to some extent, i consider myself an opponent of it. without a shadow of a doubt, there are some reasons to believe that news is not relevant to people's lives. some people just focus on the field they work in or have a keen interest in, so they do not take other information into account. for instance, if they are artists and do not have any knowledge of finance or the economy, the news of these kinds seems useless for them. thus, it is understandable why some people think the news has no connection with their life and do not need to know about some news. although some news has no advantage in their lives, most of them are so practical and directly connected to their lives. news helps people know what happens around them so they can understand some current circumstances and prepare for them. for example, in the middle of the corona pandemic, people need to update information about this disease to protect themselves. therefore, the news is useful and directly linked with people’s lives, so they should update news on a daily basis. in conclusion, although some news is unessential for some people, most of them have a direct connection to people’s lives, so it is are very necessary for people to update them.
question: Some people think news has no connection to people’s lives, so it is a waste of time to read news in the newspaper and watch news programs on television. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

essay: some today contend that high school students should have greater latitude to critique and question their teachers, despite the risks this presents for classroom management. i am strongly in agreement as this enables the ideal classroom environment. critics of this shift in mentality point out that the teacher position requires respect. a teacher who is consistently being interrupted by students will not be able to lecture and assign and mark homework well. many students might exploit a rule that encourages questions in order to intentionally derail lessons and undermine the teacher. there is also the possibility that the negative attitude of some students will create general unrest that is not conducive to learning. however, i would argue that the appearance of respect, when in reality most students question their teachers in private, holds little actual value beyond preserving unwarranted pride. moreover, the free discussion of ideas is essential to the best learning. students who are sitting quietly and feigning interest in class are likely to become less motivated. on the other hand, if a class is studying a work of literature or an event in history, students will engage more fully in lessons given the opportunity to ask questions. by asking questions, even if these queries are loosely defined as criticisms of what is being taught, they will suddenly have a reason to learn and the lesson will become more memorable for all students. the questions might also lead the class to novel insights as long as the teacher is confident enough to embark on collaborative explorations. in conclusion, although certain educational institutions fear that questions may undermine teachers, they in fact are the foundation of a positive learning environment. schools should embrace academic rigour in all its forms.
question:
Many people argue that in order to improve educational quality, high school students should be encouraged to question and offer criticisms on their teachers. Others think this will lead to a loss of respect and discipline in the classroom.

Discuss both views and give your own opinion.

essay: there has been a controversy about the purpose of museums. some people said that museums should be made to entertain people while others argue that they are supposed to be educational places. my opinion is that it will be better if museums could be both educational and entertaining. on the one hand, many people believe that the main role of museums is to entertain. museums are places where many artefacts are showcased and past events are told. that said, people can feel bored when just reading and listening to historical content. so it is important for museums to perform interesting activities and games relating to these events. for instance, they can do a quiz about historical events and include rewards for the right answers. that way people will definitely be more interested in the exhibition and they can both have fun and learn new things while doing these activities. on the other hand, it can be argued that education should be the main aim of museums. people usually do not know about the story behind those relics and past events in the exhibition. that's why documentaries and professional guides are needed to explain thoroughly to the visitors. this will give people insights into historical events and museums will play a vital role in teaching people about culture and history. to conclude, i believe that museums should offer an interesting and enjoyable experience for people while also providing them with educational knowledge as well. in that way, museums will definitely attract more visitors.
question: Some people think that museums should be enjoyable places to entertain people, while others believe that the purpose of museums is to educate. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.

essay: climate change has become one of the most dangerous threats to the existence of the human race. many opine that something should be done to combat it whilst others believe we should manage to live with it. i side with the former notion and will give some argument points to illustrate it. on the one hand, it is often argued that there is no point in stopping natural phenomena because they are out of our control. it is a matter of fact that numerous disasters such as t-sunami, earthquakes and devastating storms albeit warned about in advance are unstoppable. for example, there is nothing that can be done to fight storms in the middle part of vietnam despite their frequent occurrence. all we can do is predict them and try to limit the number of casualties to a minimum rate. hence, it is easy to see why this argument is strongly supported. on the other hand, myriad folks concede numerous human beings' activities have contributed enormously to the changes in the climate; therefore, we must be the ones taking action to prevent it from being worse. regarding individuals, each person can cease using private cars and travel by public transportation to reduce the emissions, which are the main cause of greenhouse gases, leading to global warming. in terms of the upper level, the government had better enact legislation on forcing companies and businesses to alter the way they produce waste and treat it, resulting in less polluted landfills and water sources. in general, it goes without saying that these practical methods are effective in the long run. to conclude, although many natural disasters are liable to occur beyond our will, i advocate taking steps immediately to control climate change in some aspects on the grounds that even minor changes can create a better future for our heirs. and this can be possible if individuals, the government and entrepreneurs coordinate with each other.
question: Some people think that instead of preventing climate change, we need to find a way to live with it. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

essay: advertising induces a customer towards buying a product. there is a school of thought, which, is of the opinion that the consumer's decision-making process is highly influenced by advertising , whereas,there are people who believe the contrary. in my essay, i shall discuss both views with relevant examples and would share my thoughts on the matter. first and foremost, product communication informs the product benefits. however, also creates an aspiration among the expected buyers for owning it. various forms of print, audio and visual effects are put to use, to ensure that the need of the product is created and the end user is driven towards buying the same. for example, it is the advertisements of aspiration which create brand value for apple phones, encouraging the potential purchase of the mobile. all big brands use branding as a tool to drive sales by impacting psychology. this is mostly true in consumer products and packaged goods. on the contrary, there are instances where the quality of the product speaks about itself and it is a real need of the customer, and benefits, that determine the purchasing intent. if a manufacturer is honest about quality, he does not need to advertise to influence minds, the customer will buy owing to its unwavering features and value. industrial products , and machinery , come under this category. for example, while buying tires the customer will look for the grip, texture of the surface and his experience with the product. conclusively , after discussing both the views, for and against the motion, i can conclude, that it is critical and gainful to advertise and create a positive perception of the product. the benefits if communicated properly will help probable clients to make up their minds about a product or a service. in the world of competition, it is of utmost importance to have knowledgeable and loyal customers.
question: Some people belive that advertising has a strong effect on a person's decision-making process. Other people believe that it has little or no real impact. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.

essay: art education in schools is a topic of debate, with some individuals emphasising its importance while others perceive it as a futile endeavour. this essay will examine both viewpoints and provide insights into the benefits and drawbacks of including art as a subject in the school curriculum. ultimately, it is my opinion that art is an essential subject that fosters creativity, critical thinking, and personal development. those who argue against art education often view it as a waste of time, asserting that it diverts valuable resources and attention from core academic subjects such as math and science. they believe that students should focus solely on acquiring knowledge and skills that are directly applicable in the professional world. additionally, some argue that art is subjective and lacks a concrete set of criteria for evaluation, making it an unreliable field to invest time and effort. however, proponents of art education argue that it plays a vital role in nurturing creativity and imagination in children. through art, students develop innovative thinking, problem-solving skills, and the ability to express themselves. artistic endeavours provide a platform for self-discovery and self-expression, allowing students to explore their emotions, thoughts, and perspectives. moreover, art encourages collaboration, as students often work together on projects, fostering teamwork and communication skills.

essay: many today feel that countries should prioritise vocational training over higher education due to changes in the labour market. in my opinion, though there are strong economic reasons to support this idea, university education holds greater value. those in favour of more practical skills training point out the financial benefits. most university graduates are burdened with student debt and face uncertain job prospects. in contrast, training schools are cheap, fast, and ease students into steady employment immediately. the jobs they train for are also most likely recession-proof and durable, including positions as cooks, hotel managers, and skilled technicians. someone who is firmly established in such a job in their early 20s does not have to deal with the pressures associated with university life and its incumbent debts. instead, they can begin to set aside money for a house or start a family. nonetheless, lacking a university degree limits one’s options. the jobs available will pay well initially and be secure but offer narrow scope for advancement and virtually no opportunity to switch career paths. an individual with a university degree, on the other hand, might begin from a lower position but has a higher ceiling on future earnings. it is also possible for them to explore a variety of fields. most good jobs require at least a bachelor’s degree even for simple internship vacancies. lacking such a degree, means restricting oneself to manual labour or service industry jobs. the actual learning that takes place at university, particularly for those with multi-disciplinary majors or studying at liberal arts schools, also encourages students to consider a wide range of possible career options. in conclusion, increasing funding for vocational training is an attractive yet short-sighted approach that curbs possibilities. governments ought to continue to support higher education even in dark economic times in order to reap future rewards.
question:
Nations should spend more money on skills and vocational training for practical work, rather than on university education.


To what extent do you agree or disagree?

essay: peer influence, colloquially recognized as peer pressure, stands as a pervasive dynamic within the socio-ecological framework, casting its defining imprint upon the behavioral paradigms and life circumstances of juveniles. the present exposition aspires to undertake a rigorous exegesis to proffer perspicuity on whether the liabilities intrinsic to peer influence inordinately eclipse its boons. the beneficial ramifications that emanate from positive peer pressure are unequivocal. predominantly, it operates as a galvanizing stimulant of unprecedented magnitude. when adolescents are privy to their peers achieving remarkable milestones across a diversity of domains, such as scholastic prowess or athletic endeavors, an incendiary spark of inspiration is kindled. this competitive impetus, an aspirational cascade of achievement, engenders not only personal flourishing but also orchestrates an elevation of accomplishments hitherto deemed unattainable. moreover, the profound compass of peer influence extends to the genesis of quintessential social adeptness. ongoing interaction with peers imparts a nuanced education in the fine arts of negotiation, effective communication, and the nuances of collective decision-making. these quintessential competencies stand as crucial instruments requisite for navigating the labyrinthine precincts of adulthood, enshrining their indispensability in contexts as diverse as professional environs and intricate relational matrices. however, to confine our reflection solely within the precincts of advantage is to sidestep the abiding adversities concomitant with peer influence. negative peer pressure, notably pernicious, frequently catalyzes perilous comportment among juveniles. this coersive undercurrent may compel individuals to partake in deleterious activities, encompassing substance consumption and debauched indulgences, to assimilate with their peer cohort. this ignites multifarious threats, not only to the somatic and psychological well-being of these impressionable adolescents but also to the impending specter of legal censure and social reprobation. additionally, the regulatory agency of peer influence, far from being an innocuous agent of camaraderie, possesses the latent potential to occlude individuality and extirpate personal identity. the magnetic allure of group conformity, in its more potent manifestations, potentially incites individuals to disavow their dearly held values and renounce their innate passions in the inexorable quest for conformity. the resultant repercussions include arrested self-actualization and the preclusion of the unbridled exploration of unique talents and idiosyncrasies. in summation, the variegated tapestry of peer influence, replete with contrasting facets, undeniably wields considerable sway over the comportment and vicissitudes of the adolescent cohort. while the boons intrinsic to positive peer pressure are starkly conspicuous, notably in the realms of motivation and the cultvation of pivotal social dexterity, it is incontrovertible that the attendant liabilities, prominently featuring injurious behaviors and the stifling of individuality, are equally trenchant. thus, it becomes incumbent upon the burgeoning generation to adroitly calibrate the scales of influence, ensuring that the choices they make are circumspect, fostering a trajectory of personal evolution that is both discerning and positive.

essay: in today's society, an increasing number of students are finding it more challenging to excel academically once they enter university compared to their time in school. this essay will explore the reasons behind this difficulty and propose practical solutions to help students overcome these challenges. the first reason why students may struggle with their studies at university is the higher level of academic expectations. unlike school, university demands independent learning and critical thinking skills. consequently, students who were accustomed to memorizing information in school may find it daunting to adjust to a more demanding academic environment. to overcome this, universities can implement preparatory courses or workshops to equip incoming students with necessary study skills and strategies. another factor contributing to the difficulty faced by university students is the increased workload and time management requirements. university curricula are often more extensive, with heavier reading assignments and research projects. coupled with the need to manage other responsibilities, such as part-time jobs or extracurricular activities, students may feel overwhelmed. to address this issue, universities should offer time management workshops and provide access to effective study resources, such as libraries with extended opening hours or online platforms with study tools and resources. additionally, the transition from school to university often entails a significant change in social dynamics. students may find themselves in a new city or surrounded by unfamiliar faces, which can lead to feelings of loneliness or isolation. this emotional strain can hinder their focus and motivation for studies. a solution to combat this issue would be for universities to establish mentorship programs where senior students could offer guidance and support to new students, creating a sense of belonging and fostering positive social connections. in conclusion, the challenges faced by students in university can be attributed to increased academic expectations, heavier workloads, and social adjustments. however, by implementing preparatory courses, time management workshops, and mentorship programs, universities can help students navigate these challenges effectively. it is crucial for institutions to provide the necessary resources and
